authors_text: Yunsong Zhou,Hongzi Zhu,Quan Liu,Shan Chang,Minyi Guo
title: MonoATT: Online Monocular 3D Object Detection with Adaptive Token Transformer
title_zh: MonoATT：使用自适应令牌转换器进行在线单目 3D 物体检测
comment: in the Proceedings of IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), 2023
comments: in the Proceedings of IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), 2023
abstract: Mobile monocular 3D object detection (Mono3D) (e.g., on a vehicle, a drone, or a robot) is an important yet challenging task. Existing transformer-based offline Mono3D models adopt grid-based vision tokens, which is suboptimal when using coarse tokens due to the limited available computational power. In this paper, we propose an online Mono3D framework, called MonoATT, which leverages a novel vision transformer with heterogeneous tokens of varying shapes and sizes to facilitate mobile Mono3D. The core idea of MonoATT is to adaptively assign finer tokens to areas of more significance before utilizing a transformer to enhance Mono3D. To this end, we first use prior knowledge to design a scoring network for selecting the most important areas of the image, and then propose a token clustering and merging network with an attention mechanism to gradually merge tokens around the selected areas in multiple stages. Finally, a pixel-level feature map is reconstructed from heterogeneous tokens before employing a SOTA Mono3D detector as the underlying detection core. Experiment results on the real-world KITTI dataset demonstrate that MonoATT can effectively improve the Mono3D accuracy for both near and far objects and guarantee low latency. MonoATT yields the best performance compared with the state-of-the-art methods by a large margin and is ranked number one on the KITTI 3D benchmark.

Existing transformer-based offline Mono3D models adopt grid-based vision tokens, which is suboptimal when using coarse tokens due to the limited available computational power. The core idea of MonoATT is to adaptively assign finer tokens to areas of more significance before utilizing a transformer to enhance Mono3D. To this end, we first use prior knowledge to design a scoring network for selecting the most important areas of the image, and then propose a token clustering and merging network with an attention mechanism to gradually merge tokens around the selected areas in multiple stages. Finally, a pixel-level feature map is reconstructed from heterogeneous tokens before employing a SOTA Mono3D detector as the underlying detection core. Experiment results on the real-world KITTI dataset demonstrate that MonoATT can effectively improve the Mono3D accuracy for both near and far objects and guarantee low latency. MonoATT yields the best performance compared with the state-of-the-art methods by a large margin and is ranked number one on the KITTI 3D benchmark.
abstract_zh: 移动单目 3D 物体检测 (Mono3D)（例如，在车辆、无人机或机器人上）是一项重要但具有挑战性的任务。现有的基于 Transformer 的离线 Mono3D 模型采用基于网格的视觉令牌，由于可用计算能力有限，因此在使用粗令牌时效果不佳。在本文中，我们提出了一个名为 MonoATT 的在线 Mono3D 框架，它利用具有不同形状和大小的异构令牌的新颖视觉转换器来促进移动 Mono3D。 MonoATT 的核心思想是在利用 Transformer 增强 Mono3D 之前，自适应地将更精细的标记分配给更重要的区域。为此，我们首先利用先验知识设计一个评分网络来选择图像中最重要的区域，然后提出一个具有注意机制的令牌聚类和合并网络，以分多个阶段逐步合并所选区域周围的令牌。最后，在采用 SOTA Mono3D 检测器作为底层检测核心之前，从异构标记重建像素级特征图。在现实世界的 KITTI 数据集上的实验结果表明，MonoATT 可以有效提高近处和远处物体的 Mono3D 精度，并保证低延迟。与最先进的方法相比，MonoATT 的性能大幅提升，并且在 KITTI 3D 基准测试中排名第一。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为MonoATT的在线单目3D目标检测框架，通过自适应令牌变换器提升移动设备上的检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有基于Transformer的离线单目3D检测模型采用网格化视觉令牌，因计算资源有限导致粗粒度令牌表现不佳，难以满足移动设备需求。
AI.method: 设计评分网络筛选图像关键区域，结合令牌聚类与合并网络分阶段融合异构令牌，并重建像素级特征图以驱动先进检测器。
AI.result: 在KITTI数据集上，MonoATT在近远距离目标检测精度和延迟表现均优于现有方法，位列3D基准榜首。
AI.conclusion: MonoATT通过自适应令牌机制有效平衡了检测精度与计算效率，为移动单目3D检测提供了新范式。

authors_text: Elijah Wyckoff,Ronan Reza,Fernando Moreu
title: Feedback and Control of Dynamics and Robotics using Augmented Reality
title_zh: 使用增强现实对动力学和机器人进行反馈和控制
comment: 19 pages, 17 figures, 1 table
comments: 19 pages, 17 figures, 1 table
abstract: Human-machine interaction (HMI) and human-robot interaction (HRI) can assist structural monitoring and structural dynamics testing in the laboratory and field. In vibratory experimentation, one mode of generating vibration is to use electrodynamic exciters. Manual control is a common way of setting the input of the exciter by the operator. To measure the structural responses to these generated vibrations sensors are attached to the structure. These sensors can be deployed by repeatable robots with high endurance, which require on-the-fly control. If the interface between operators and the controls was augmented, then operators can visualize the experiments, exciter levels, and define robot input with a better awareness of the area of interest. Robots can provide better aid to humans if intelligent on-the-fly control of the robot is: (1) quantified and presented to the human; (2) conducted in real-time for human feedback informed by data. Information provided by the new interface would be used to change the control input based on their understanding of real-time parameters. This research proposes using Augmented Reality (AR) applications to provide humans with sensor feedback and control of actuators and robots. This method improves cognition by allowing the operator to maintain awareness of structures while adjusting conditions accordingly with the assistance of the new real-time interface. One interface application is developed to plot sensor data in addition to voltage, frequency, and duration controls for vibration generation. Two more applications are developed under similar framework, one to control the position of a mediating robot and one to control the frequency of the robot movement. This paper presents the proposed model for the new control loop and then compares the new approach with a traditional method by measuring time delay in control input and user efficiency.

abstract_zh: 人机交互（HMI）和人机交互（HRI）可以协助实验室和现场的结构监测和结构动力学测试。在振动实验中，产生振动的一种模式是使用电动激振器。手动控制是操作员设置励磁机输入的常用方法。为了测量结构对这些产生的振动的响应，将传感器安装在结构上。这些传感器可以由具有高耐用性的可重复机器人部署，这需要动态控制。如果操作员和控制器之间的界面得到增强，那么操作员可以可视化实验、激励器级别，并定义机器人输入，更好地了解感兴趣的区域。如果机器人的智能动态控制满足以下条件，则机器人可以为人类提供更好的帮助：（1）量化并呈现给人类； (2)通过数据实时进行人类反馈。新界面提供的信息将用于根据他们对实时参数的理解来改变控制输入。这项研究建议使用增强现实（AR）应用程序为人类提供传感器反馈以及执行器和机器人的控制。该方法允许操作员保持对结构的认识，同时在新的实时界面的帮助下相应地调整条件，从而提高认知能力。开发了一种界面应用程序来绘制传感器数据以及振动产生的电压、频率和持续时间控制。在类似的框架下还开发了另外两个应用程序，一个用于控制中介机器人的位置，另一个用于控制机器人运动的频率。本文提出了新控制环路的模型，然后通过测量控制输入的时间延迟和用户效率将新方法与传统方法进行比较。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种使用增强现实（AR）技术来改进人机交互和人机控制的方法，用于结构动力学实验和机器人控制。
AI.motivation: 在结构动力学实验中，人工控制和传感器数据反馈存在效率低、实时性差的问题，需要一种更直观和实时的控制方式。
AI.method: 本文开发了基于AR的应用程序，用于可视化传感器数据并实时控制振动发生器、中介机器人和机器人移动频率，以改善操作员的认知和控制效率。
AI.result: 通过比较新方法与传统方法，发现新方法在控制输入延迟和用户效率方面有所提升。
AI.conclusion: AR技术可以有效增强人机交互和机器人控制，提高结构动力学实验的实时性和效率。

authors_text: Marc Katzef,Andrew C. Cullen,Tansu Alpcan,Christopher Leckie,Justin Kopacz
title: Failure-tolerant Distributed Learning for Anomaly Detection in Wireless Networks
title_zh: 用于无线网络异常检测的容错分布式学习
abstract: The analysis of distributed techniques is often focused upon their efficiency, without considering their robustness (or lack thereof). Such a consideration is particularly important when devices or central servers can fail, which can potentially cripple distributed systems. When such failures arise in wireless communications networks, important services that they use/provide (like anomaly detection) can be left inoperable and can result in a cascade of security problems. In this paper, we present a novel method to address these risks by combining both flat- and star-topologies, combining the performance and reliability benefits of both. We refer to this method as "Tol-FL", due to its increased failure-tolerance as compared to the technique of Federated Learning. Our approach both limits device failure risks while outperforming prior methods by up to 8% in terms of anomaly detection AUROC in a range of realistic settings that consider client as well as server failure, all while reducing communication costs. This performance demonstrates that Tol-FL is a highly suitable method for distributed model training for anomaly detection, especially in the domain of wireless networks.
summary: The analysis of distributed techniques is often focused upon their efficiency, without considering their robustness (or lack thereof). Such a consideration is particularly important when devices or central servers can fail, which can potentially cripple distributed systems. When such failures arise in wireless communications networks, important services that they use/provide (like anomaly detection) can be left inoperable and can result in a cascade of security problems. In this paper, we present a novel method to address these risks by combining both flat- and star-topologies, combining the performance and reliability benefits of both. We refer to this method as "Tol-FL", due to its increased failure-tolerance as compared to the technique of Federated Learning. Our approach both limits device failure risks while outperforming prior methods by up to 8% in terms of anomaly detection AUROC in a range of realistic settings that consider client as well as server failure, all while reducing communication costs. This performance demonstrates that Tol-FL is a highly suitable method for distributed model training for anomaly detection, especially in the domain of wireless networks.
abstract_zh: 对分布式技术的分析通常侧重于其效率，而不考虑其鲁棒性（或缺乏鲁棒性）。当设备或中央服务器可能发生故障时，这种考虑尤其重要，这可能会削弱分布式系统。当无线通信网络中出现此类故障时，它们使用/提供的重要服务（例如异常检测）可能无法运行，并可能导致一系列安全问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的方法来解决这些风险，通过结合扁平和星形拓扑，结合两者的性能和可靠性优势。我们将此方法称为“Tol-FL”，因为与联邦学习技术相比，它具有更高的容错能力。我们的方法既限制了设备故障风险，又在考虑客户端和服务器故障的一系列实际设置中，在异常检测 AUROC 方面优于先前方法高达 8%，同时降低了通信成本。这一性能表明，Tol-FL 是一种非常适合异常检测分布式模型训练的方法，尤其是在无线网络领域。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合扁平化与星型拓扑结构的容错分布式学习方法Tol-FL，用于提升无线网络异常检测的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有分布式技术过度关注效率而忽视鲁棒性，设备或服务器故障可能导致异常检测服务中断并引发安全问题。
AI.method: 通过融合扁平化与星型拓扑结构设计Tol-FL框架，在保证性能的同时提升系统容错能力。
AI.result: 在多种真实场景下，Tol-FL相较现有方法异常检测AUROC指标提升8%，同时降低通信成本。
AI.conclusion: Tol-FL为无线网络环境下的分布式模型训练提供了高效且容错的解决方案，尤其适用于对可靠性要求高的场景。

authors_text: Fangfu Liu,Chubin Zhang,Yu Zheng,Yueqi Duan
title: Semantic Ray: Learning a Generalizable Semantic Field with Cross-Reprojection Attention
title_zh: 语义射线：通过交叉重投影注意力学习可概括的语义场
comment: Accepted by CVPR 2023. Project page: https://liuff19.github.io/S-Ray/
comments: Accepted by CVPR 2023. Project page: https://liuff19.github.io/S-Ray/
abstract: In this paper, we aim to learn a semantic radiance field from multiple scenes that is accurate, efficient and generalizable. While most existing NeRFs target at the tasks of neural scene rendering, image synthesis and multi-view reconstruction, there are a few attempts such as Semantic-NeRF that explore to learn high-level semantic understanding with the NeRF structure. However, Semantic-NeRF simultaneously learns color and semantic label from a single ray with multiple heads, where the single ray fails to provide rich semantic information. As a result, Semantic NeRF relies on positional encoding and needs to train one specific model for each scene. To address this, we propose Semantic Ray (S-Ray) to fully exploit semantic information along the ray direction from its multi-view reprojections. As directly performing dense attention over multi-view reprojected rays would suffer from heavy computational cost, we design a Cross-Reprojection Attention module with consecutive intra-view radial and cross-view sparse attentions, which decomposes contextual information along reprojected rays and cross multiple views and then collects dense connections by stacking the modules. Experiments show that our S-Ray is able to learn from multiple scenes, and it presents strong generalization ability to adapt to unseen scenes.
summary: In this paper, we aim to learn a semantic radiance field from multiple scenes that is accurate, efficient and generalizable. While most existing NeRFs target at the tasks of neural scene rendering, image synthesis and multi-view reconstruction, there are a few attempts such as Semantic-NeRF that explore to learn high-level semantic understanding with the NeRF structure. However, Semantic-NeRF simultaneously learns color and semantic label from a single ray with multiple heads, where the single ray fails to provide rich semantic information. As a result, Semantic NeRF relies on positional encoding and needs to train one specific model for each scene. To address this, we propose Semantic Ray (S-Ray) to fully exploit semantic information along the ray direction from its multi-view reprojections. As directly performing dense attention over multi-view reprojected rays would suffer from heavy computational cost, we design a Cross-Reprojection Attention module with consecutive intra-view radial and cross-view sparse attentions, which decomposes contextual information along reprojected rays and cross multiple views and then collects dense connections by stacking the modules. Experiments show that our S-Ray is able to learn from multiple scenes, and it presents strong generalization ability to adapt to unseen scenes.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们的目标是从多个场景中学习准确、高效且可推广的语义辐射场。虽然大多数现有的 NeRF 针对神经场景渲染、图像合成和多视图重建的任务，但也有一些尝试，例如 Semantic-NeRF，探索利用 NeRF 结构学习高级语义理解。然而，Semantic-NeRF 同时从具有多个头的单条射线学习颜色和语义标签，其中单条射线无法提供丰富的语义信息。因此，Semantic NeRF 依赖于位置编码，需要为每个场景训练一个特定模型。为了解决这个问题，我们提出语义射线（S-Ray），从多视图重投影中充分利用沿射线方向的语义信息。由于直接对多视图重投影光线执行密集注意力会产生大量计算成本，因此我们设计了一个具有连续视图内径向和跨视图稀疏注意力的交叉重投影注意力模块，该模块沿着重投影光线分解上下文信息并跨多个视图，然后通过堆叠模块来收集密集连接。实验表明，我们的 S-Ray 能够从多个场景中学习，并且具有很强的泛化能力，可以适应未见过的场景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Semantic Ray（S-Ray）的方法，旨在从多个场景中学习准确、高效且具有泛化能力的语义辐射场。
AI.motivation: 现有方法如Semantic-NeRF通过单条射线同时学习颜色和语义标签，但单条射线语义信息不足，导致依赖位置编码且需为每个场景单独训练模型。为解决此问题，本文提出利用多视角重投影射线的语义信息。
AI.method: 设计了Cross-Reprojection Attention模块，通过连续的视图内径向注意力和跨视图稀疏注意力分解重投影射线的上下文信息，并通过堆叠模块建立密集连接，降低计算成本。
AI.result: 实验表明，S-Ray能够从多个场景中学习，并在未见过的场景中展现出强大的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: S-Ray通过多视角重投影与注意力机制有效利用语义信息，无需为每个场景单独训练模型，实现了跨场景的强泛化性能。

authors_text: Nan Gao,Zeyu Zhao,Zhi Zeng,Shuwu Zhang,Dongdong Weng,Yihua Bao
title: GesGPT: Speech Gesture Synthesis With Text Parsing from ChatGPT
title_zh: GesGPT：通过 ChatGPT 进行文本解析的语音手势合成
comment: ef:IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters 9 (2024) 3
comments: ef:IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters 9 (2024) 3
abstract: Gesture synthesis has gained significant attention as a critical research field, aiming to produce contextually appropriate and natural gestures corresponding to speech or textual input. Although deep learning-based approaches have achieved remarkable progress, they often overlook the rich semantic information present in the text, leading to less expressive and meaningful gestures. In this letter, we propose GesGPT, a novel approach to gesture generation that leverages the semantic analysis capabilities of large language models , such as ChatGPT. By capitalizing on the strengths of LLMs for text analysis, we adopt a controlled approach to generate and integrate professional gestures and base gestures through a text parsing script, resulting in diverse and meaningful gestures. Firstly, our approach involves the development of prompt principles that transform gesture generation into an intention classification problem using ChatGPT. We also conduct further analysis on emphasis words and semantic words to aid in gesture generation. Subsequently, we construct a specialized gesture lexicon with multiple semantic annotations, decoupling the synthesis of gestures into professional gestures and base gestures. Finally, we merge the professional gestures with base gestures. Experimental results demonstrate that GesGPT effectively generates contextually appropriate and expressive gestures.
summary: Gesture synthesis has gained significant attention as a critical research field, aiming to produce contextually appropriate and natural gestures corresponding to speech or textual input. Although deep learning-based approaches have achieved remarkable progress, they often overlook the rich semantic information present in the text, leading to less expressive and meaningful gestures. In this letter, we propose GesGPT, a novel approach to gesture generation that leverages the semantic analysis capabilities of large language models , such as ChatGPT. By capitalizing on the strengths of LLMs for text analysis, we adopt a controlled approach to generate and integrate professional gestures and base gestures through a text parsing script, resulting in diverse and meaningful gestures. Firstly, our approach involves the development of prompt principles that transform gesture generation into an intention classification problem using ChatGPT. We also conduct further analysis on emphasis words and semantic words to aid in gesture generation. Subsequently, we construct a specialized gesture lexicon with multiple semantic annotations, decoupling the synthesis of gestures into professional gestures and base gestures. Finally, we merge the professional gestures with base gestures. Experimental results demonstrate that GesGPT effectively generates contextually appropriate and expressive gestures.
abstract_zh: 手势合成作为一个关键研究领域已经获得了极大的关注，旨在产生与语音或文本输入相对应的上下文适当且自然的手势。尽管基于深度学习的方法取得了显着的进步，但它们经常忽略文本中存在的丰富语义信息，导致手势表达力和意义较差。在这封信中，我们提出了 GesGPT，一种利用大型语言模型（例如 ChatGPT）的语义分析功能的手势生成新方法。通过利用法学硕士在文本分析方面的优势，我们采用受控方法通过文本解析脚本生成和集成专业手势和基础手势，从而产生多样化且有意义的手势。首先，我们的方法涉及开发提示原则，使用 ChatGPT 将手势生成转换为意图分类问题。我们还对强调词和语义词进行进一步分析，以帮助手势生成。随后，我们构建了一个具有多个语义注释的专门手势词典，将手势合成解耦为专业手势和基础手势。最后，我们将专业手势与基本手势合并。实验结果表明，GesGPT 可以有效地生成适合上下文且富有表现力的手势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出GesGPT方法，通过ChatGPT的文本解析能力实现语音手势合成。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习方法在手势生成中未能充分利用文本的语义信息，导致生成手势表达力不足。
AI.method: 利用LLM进行文本语义分析，通过提示原则将手势生成转化为意图分类问题，构建包含语义标注的专业手势词典，并融合基础手势与专业手势。
AI.result: 实验表明GesGPT能有效生成符合上下文且富有表现力的手势。

search_text: GesGPT: Speech Gesture Synthesis With Text Parsing from ChatGPT GesGPT：通过 ChatGPT 进行文本解析的语音手势合成 Gesture synthesis has gained significant attention as a critical research field, aiming to produce contextually appropriate and natural gestures corresponding to speech or textual input. Although deep learning-based approaches have achieved remarkable progress, they often overlook the rich semantic information present in the text, leading to less expressive and meaningful gestures. In this letter, we propose GesGPT, a novel approach to gesture generation that leverages the semantic analysis capabilities of large language models , such as ChatGPT. By capitalizing on the strengths of LLMs for text analysis, we adopt a controlled approach to generate and integrate professional gestures and base gestures through a text parsing script, resulting in diverse and meaningful gestures. Firstly, our approach involves the development of prompt principles that transform gesture generation into an intention classification problem using ChatGPT. We also conduct further analysis on emphasis words and semantic words to aid in gesture generation. Subsequently, we construct a specialized gesture lexicon with multiple semantic annotations, decoupling the synthesis of gestures into professional gestures and base gestures. Finally, we merge the professional gestures with base gestures. Experimental results demonstrate that GesGPT effectively generates contextually appropriate and expressive gestures. 手势合成作为一个关键研究领域已经获得了极大的关注，旨在产生与语音或文本输入相对应的上下文适当且自然的手势。尽管基于深度学习的方法取得了显着的进步，但它们经常忽略文本中存在的丰富语义信息，导致手势表达力和意义较差。在这封信中，我们提出了 GesGPT，一种利用大型语言模型（例如 ChatGPT）的语义分析功能的手势生成新方法。通过利用法学硕士在文本分析方面的优势，我们采用受控方法通过文本解析脚本生成和集成专业手势和基础手势，从而产生多样化且有意义的手势。首先，我们的方法涉及开发提示原则，使用 ChatGPT 将手势生成转换为意图分类问题。我们还对强调词和语义词进行进一步分析，以帮助手势生成。随后，我们构建了一个具有多个语义注释的专门手势词典，将手势合成解耦为专业手势和基础手势。最后，我们将专业手势与基本手势合并。实验结果表明，GesGPT 可以有效地生成适合上下文且富有表现力的手势。 本文提出GesGPT方法，通过ChatGPT的文本解析能力实现语音手势合成。 Nan Gao,Zeyu Zhao,Zhi Zeng,Shuwu Zhang,Dongdong Weng,Yihua Bao cs.CL,cs.CV,cs.HC

authors_text: Jinqi Luo,Zhaoning Wang,Chen Henry Wu,Dong Huang,Fernando De la Torre
title: Semantic Image Attack for Visual Model Diagnosis
title_zh: 用于视觉模型诊断的语义图像攻击
comment: Initial version submitted to NeurIPS 2022
comments: Initial version submitted to NeurIPS 2022
abstract: In practice, metric analysis on a specific train and test dataset does not guarantee reliable or fair ML models. This is partially due to the fact that obtaining a balanced, diverse, and perfectly labeled dataset is typically expensive, time-consuming, and error-prone. Rather than relying on a carefully designed test set to assess ML models' failures, fairness, or robustness, this paper proposes Semantic Image Attack (SIA), a method based on the adversarial attack that provides semantic adversarial images to allow model diagnosis, interpretability, and robustness. Traditional adversarial training is a popular methodology for robustifying ML models against attacks. However, existing adversarial methods do not combine the two aspects that enable the interpretation and analysis of the model's flaws: semantic traceability and perceptual quality. SIA combines the two features via iterative gradient ascent on a predefined semantic attribute space and the image space. We illustrate the validity of our approach in three scenarios for keypoint detection and classification. (1) Model diagnosis: SIA generates a histogram of attributes that highlights the semantic vulnerability of the ML model (i.e., attributes that make the model fail). (2) Stronger attacks: SIA generates adversarial examples with visually interpretable attributes that lead to higher attack success rates than baseline methods. The adversarial training on SIA improves the transferable robustness across different gradient-based attacks. (3) Robustness to imbalanced datasets: we use SIA to augment the underrepresented classes, which outperforms strong augmentation and re-balancing baselines.
summary: In practice, metric analysis on a specific train and test dataset does not guarantee reliable or fair ML models. This is partially due to the fact that obtaining a balanced, diverse, and perfectly labeled dataset is typically expensive, time-consuming, and error-prone. Rather than relying on a carefully designed test set to assess ML models' failures, fairness, or robustness, this paper proposes Semantic Image Attack (SIA), a method based on the adversarial attack that provides semantic adversarial images to allow model diagnosis, interpretability, and robustness. Traditional adversarial training is a popular methodology for robustifying ML models against attacks. However, existing adversarial methods do not combine the two aspects that enable the interpretation and analysis of the model's flaws: semantic traceability and perceptual quality. SIA combines the two features via iterative gradient ascent on a predefined semantic attribute space and the image space. We illustrate the validity of our approach in three scenarios for keypoint detection and classification. (1) Model diagnosis: SIA generates a histogram of attributes that highlights the semantic vulnerability of the ML model (i.e., attributes that make the model fail). (2) Stronger attacks: SIA generates adversarial examples with visually interpretable attributes that lead to higher attack success rates than baseline methods. The adversarial training on SIA improves the transferable robustness across different gradient-based attacks. (3) Robustness to imbalanced datasets: we use SIA to augment the underrepresented classes, which outperforms strong augmentation and re-balancing baselines.
abstract_zh: 在实践中，对特定训练和测试数据集的度量分析并不能保证机器学习模型的可靠或公平。部分原因是获得平衡、多样化和完美标记的数据集通常是昂贵、耗时且容易出错的。本文提出了语义图像攻击（SIA），而不是依靠精心设计的测试集来评估 ML 模型的失败、公平性或鲁棒性，这是一种基于对抗性攻击的方法，提供语义对抗性图像以允许模型诊断、可解释性和鲁棒性。传统的对抗训练是增强机器学习模型抵御攻击的流行方法。然而，现有的对抗方法没有结合能够解释和分析模型缺陷的两个方面：语义可追溯性和感知质量。 SIA 通过在预定义的语义属性空间和图像空间上迭代梯度上升来组合这两个特征。我们在关键点检测和分类的三种场景中说明了我们的方法的有效性。 (1) 模型诊断：SIA 生成属性直方图，突出显示 ML 模型的语义漏洞（即导致模型失败的属性）。 (2) 更强的攻击：SIA 生成具有视觉可解释属性的对抗性示例，与基线方法相比，攻击成功率更高。 SIA 的对抗训练提高了不同基于梯度的攻击的可转移鲁棒性。 (3) 对不平衡数据集的鲁棒性：我们使用 SIA 来增强代表性不足的类别，这优于强增强和重新平衡基线。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于语义图像攻击（SIA）的方法，用于诊断视觉模型的缺陷并提升其鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 传统数据集难以保证模型可靠性与公平性，且依赖精心设计的测试集存在局限。现有对抗方法缺乏语义可解释性与感知质量结合的分析能力。
AI.method: 通过预定义语义属性空间与图像空间的迭代梯度上升，生成具有语义可解释性的对抗样本，实现模型诊断与鲁棒性增强。
AI.result: SIA在关键点检测与分类任务中有效：1) 生成属性直方图揭示模型语义漏洞；2) 攻击成功率高于基线方法；3) 增强对不平衡数据集的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: SIA通过结合语义可追溯性与感知质量，为模型诊断和对抗训练提供了有效工具，优于现有增强方法。

authors_text: Dohwan Ko,Joonmyung Choi,Hyeong Kyu Choi,Kyoung-Woon On,Byungseok Roh,Hyunwoo J. Kim
title: MELTR: Meta Loss Transformer for Learning to Fine-tune Video Foundation Models
title_zh: MELTR：用于学习微调视频基础模型的元损失变压器
comment: Accepted paper at CVPR 2023
comments: Accepted paper at CVPR 2023
abstract: Foundation models have shown outstanding performance and generalization capabilities across domains. Since most studies on foundation models mainly focus on the pretraining phase, a naive strategy to minimize a single task-specific loss is adopted for fine-tuning. However, such fine-tuning methods do not fully leverage other losses that are potentially beneficial for the target task. Therefore, we propose MEta Loss TRansformer (MELTR), a plug-in module that automatically and non-linearly combines various loss functions to aid learning the target task via auxiliary learning. We formulate the auxiliary learning as a bi-level optimization problem and present an efficient optimization algorithm based on Approximate Implicit Differentiation (AID). For evaluation, we apply our framework to various video foundation models (UniVL, Violet and All-in-one), and show significant performance gain on all four downstream tasks: text-to-video retrieval, video question answering, video captioning, and multi-modal sentiment analysis. Our qualitative analyses demonstrate that MELTR adequately `transforms' individual loss functions and `melts' them into an effective unified loss. Code is available at https://github.com/mlvlab/MELTR.
summary: Foundation models have shown outstanding performance and generalization capabilities across domains. Since most studies on foundation models mainly focus on the pretraining phase, a naive strategy to minimize a single task-specific loss is adopted for fine-tuning. However, such fine-tuning methods do not fully leverage other losses that are potentially beneficial for the target task. Therefore, we propose MEta Loss TRansformer (MELTR), a plug-in module that automatically and non-linearly combines various loss functions to aid learning the target task via auxiliary learning. We formulate the auxiliary learning as a bi-level optimization problem and present an efficient optimization algorithm based on Approximate Implicit Differentiation (AID). For evaluation, we apply our framework to various video foundation models (UniVL, Violet and All-in-one), and show significant performance gain on all four downstream tasks: text-to-video retrieval, video question answering, video captioning, and multi-modal sentiment analysis. Our qualitative analyses demonstrate that MELTR adequately `transforms' individual loss functions and `melts' them into an effective unified loss. Code is available at https://github.com/mlvlab/MELTR.
abstract_zh: 基础模型在跨领域表现出了出色的性能和泛化能力。由于大多数基础模型的研究主要集中在预训练阶段，因此采用最小化单个任务特定损失的朴素策略进行微调。然而，这种微调方法并没有充分利用对目标任务可能有利的其他损失。因此，我们提出了 MEta Loss TRansformer (MELTR)，这是一个插件模块，可以自动非线性地组合各种损失函数，以通过辅助学习来帮助学习目标任务。我们将辅助学习表述为双层优化问题，并提出一种基于近似隐式微分（AID）的有效优化算法。为了进行评估，我们将我们的框架应用于各种视频基础模型（UniVL、Violet 和 All-in-one），并在所有四个下游任务上显示出显着的性能提升：文本到视频检索、视频问答、视频字幕和多模态情感分析。我们的定性分析表明，MELTR 充分“转变”了个体损失函数，并将其“融合”为有效的统一损失。代码可在 https://github.com/mlvlab/MELTR 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MELTR（MEta Loss TRansformer），通过自动组合多种损失函数来优化视频基础模型的微调过程。
AI.motivation: 现有微调方法仅依赖单一任务损失函数，未能充分利用其他潜在有益的损失函数，限制了模型性能。
AI.method: 设计MELTR模块将辅助学习建模为双层优化问题，采用近似隐式微分（AID）算法实现多损失函数的非线性组合。
AI.result: 在UniVL、Violet等视频基础模型上，MELTR在文本-视频检索、视频问答等四个下游任务中均取得显著性能提升。
AI.conclusion: MELTR通过有效融合多损失函数，证明了辅助学习在视频基础模型微调中的价值，为模型优化提供新思路。

tldr: 本文提出MELTR（MEta Loss TRansformer），通过自动组合多种损失函数来优化视频基础模型的微调过程。
motivation: 现有微调方法仅依赖单一任务损失函数，未能充分利用其他潜在有益的损失函数，限制了模型性能。
method: 设计MELTR模块将辅助学习建模为双层优化问题，采用近似隐式微分（AID）算法实现多损失函数的非线性组合。
result: 在UniVL、Violet等视频基础模型上，MELTR在文本-视频检索、视频问答等四个下游任务中均取得显著性能提升。
conclusion: MELTR通过有效融合多损失函数，证明了辅助学习在视频基础模型微调中的价值，为模型优化提供新思路。
search_text: MELTR: Meta Loss Transformer for Learning to Fine-tune Video Foundation Models MELTR：用于学习微调视频基础模型的元损失变压器 Foundation models have shown outstanding performance and generalization capabilities across domains. Since most studies on foundation models mainly focus on the pretraining phase, a naive strategy to minimize a single task-specific loss is adopted for fine-tuning. However, such fine-tuning methods do not fully leverage other losses that are potentially beneficial for the target task. Therefore, we propose MEta Loss TRansformer (MELTR), a plug-in module that automatically and non-linearly combines various loss functions to aid learning the target task via auxiliary learning. We formulate the auxiliary learning as a bi-level optimization problem and present an efficient optimization algorithm based on Approximate Implicit Differentiation (AID). For evaluation, we apply our framework to various video foundation models (UniVL, Violet and All-in-one), and show significant performance gain on all four downstream tasks: text-to-video retrieval, video question answering, video captioning, and multi-modal sentiment analysis. Our qualitative analyses demonstrate that MELTR adequately `transforms' individual loss functions and `melts' them into an effective unified loss. Code is available at https://github.com/mlvlab/MELTR. 基础模型在跨领域表现出了出色的性能和泛化能力。由于大多数基础模型的研究主要集中在预训练阶段，因此采用最小化单个任务特定损失的朴素策略进行微调。然而，这种微调方法并没有充分利用对目标任务可能有利的其他损失。因此，我们提出了 MEta Loss TRansformer (MELTR)，这是一个插件模块，可以自动非线性地组合各种损失函数，以通过辅助学习来帮助学习目标任务。我们将辅助学习表述为双层优化问题，并提出一种基于近似隐式微分（AID）的有效优化算法。为了进行评估，我们将我们的框架应用于各种视频基础模型（UniVL、Violet 和 All-in-one），并在所有四个下游任务上显示出显着的性能提升：文本到视频检索、视频问答、视频字幕和多模态情感分析。我们的定性分析表明，MELTR 充分“转变”了个体损失函数，并将其“融合”为有效的统一损失。代码可在 https://github.com/mlvlab/MELTR 获取。 本文提出MELTR（MEta Loss TRansformer），通过自动组合多种损失函数来优化视频基础模型的微调过程。 Dohwan Ko,Joonmyung Choi,Hyeong Kyu Choi,Kyoung-Woon On,Byungseok Roh,Hyunwoo J. Kim cs.CV

authors_text: Manuel Kansy,Anton Raël,Graziana Mignone,Jacek Naruniec,Christopher Schroers,Markus Gross,Romann M. Weber
title: Controllable Inversion of Black-Box Face Recognition Models via Diffusion
title_zh: 通过扩散控制黑盒人脸识别模型的反转
comment: 8 pages main paper + 23 pages supplementary material. Moderate revisions from v1 (different template, added user study, wording). Presented at AMFG workshop at ICCV 2023. Project page: https://studios.disneyresearch.com/2023/10/02/controllable-inversion-of-black-box-face-recognition-models-via-diffusion/
comments: 8 pages main paper + 23 pages supplementary material. Moderate revisions from v1 (different template, added user study, wording). Presented at AMFG workshop at ICCV 2023. Project page: https://studios.disneyresearch.com/2023/10/02/controllable-inversion-of-black-box-face-recognition-models-via-diffusion/
abstract: Face recognition models embed a face image into a low-dimensional identity vector containing abstract encodings of identity-specific facial features that allow individuals to be distinguished from one another. We tackle the challenging task of inverting the latent space of pre-trained face recognition models without full model access (i.e. black-box setting). A variety of methods have been proposed in literature for this task, but they have serious shortcomings such as a lack of realistic outputs and strong requirements for the data set and accessibility of the face recognition model. By analyzing the black-box inversion problem, we show that the conditional diffusion model loss naturally emerges and that we can effectively sample from the inverse distribution even without an identity-specific loss. Our method, named identity denoising diffusion probabilistic model (ID3PM), leverages the stochastic nature of the denoising diffusion process to produce high-quality, identity-preserving face images with various backgrounds, lighting, poses, and expressions. We demonstrate state-of-the-art performance in terms of identity preservation and diversity both qualitatively and quantitatively, and our method is the first black-box face recognition model inversion method that offers intuitive control over the generation process.
summary: Face recognition models embed a face image into a low-dimensional identity vector containing abstract encodings of identity-specific facial features that allow individuals to be distinguished from one another. We tackle the challenging task of inverting the latent space of pre-trained face recognition models without full model access (i.e. black-box setting). A variety of methods have been proposed in literature for this task, but they have serious shortcomings such as a lack of realistic outputs and strong requirements for the data set and accessibility of the face recognition model. By analyzing the black-box inversion problem, we show that the conditional diffusion model loss naturally emerges and that we can effectively sample from the inverse distribution even without an identity-specific loss. Our method, named identity denoising diffusion probabilistic model (ID3PM), leverages the stochastic nature of the denoising diffusion process to produce high-quality, identity-preserving face images with various backgrounds, lighting, poses, and expressions. We demonstrate state-of-the-art performance in terms of identity preservation and diversity both qualitatively and quantitatively, and our method is the first black-box face recognition model inversion method that offers intuitive control over the generation process.
abstract_zh: 人脸识别模型将人脸图像嵌入到低维身份向量中，其中包含特定于身份的面部特征的抽象编码，使个体能够彼此区分。我们解决了在没有完整模型访问（即黑盒设置）的情况下反转预训练人脸识别模型的潜在空间的挑战性任务。文献中针对此任务提出了多种方法，但它们都存在严重的缺点，例如缺乏真实的输出以及对数据集和人脸识别模型的可访问性的强烈要求。通过分析黑盒反演问题，我们表明条件扩散模型损失自然出现，并且即使没有特定于身份的损失，我们也可以有效地从反分布中采样。我们的方法称为身份去噪扩散概率模型 (ID3PM)，利用去噪扩散过程的随机性来生成具有各种背景、光照、姿势和表情的高质量、身份保留的人脸图像。我们在定性和定量方面展示了身份保存和多样性方面最先进的性能，并且我们的方法是第一个黑盒人脸识别模型反演方法，可以对生成过程提供直观的控制。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种在不完全访问预训练人脸识别模型的情况下，通过扩散模型实现可控逆向生成人脸图像的方法。
AI.motivation: 现有的黑盒人脸识别模型逆向方法存在输出不够真实、对数据集和模型访问要求高的问题，因此需要一种更有效且直观控制生成过程的方法。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种名为ID3PM的身份去噪扩散概率模型，利用扩散模型的随机性，生成高质量、身份保持的人脸图像，并在不使用身份特定损失函数的情况下有效采样逆向分布。
AI.result: 在身份保持和多样性方面，本文方法在定性和定量上均达到最先进的性能，并且是第一个提供直观控制生成过程的黑盒人脸识别模型逆向方法。
AI.conclusion: ID3PM方法在黑盒人脸识别模型逆向任务中表现出色，能够生成高质量、身份保持且具有多样性的图像，为相关研究提供了新的思路和方法。

authors_text: Zhendong Yang,Ailing Zeng,Zhe Li,Tianke Zhang,Chun Yuan,Yu Li
title: From Knowledge Distillation to Self-Knowledge Distillation: A Unified Approach with Normalized Loss and Customized Soft Labels
title_zh: 从知识蒸馏到自我知识蒸馏：具有归一化损失和定制软标签的统一方法
comment: Accepted by ICCV 2023
comments: Accepted by ICCV 2023
abstract: Knowledge Distillation (KD) uses the teacher's prediction logits as soft labels to guide the student, while self-KD does not need a real teacher to require the soft labels. This work unifies the formulations of the two tasks by decomposing and reorganizing the generic KD loss into a Normalized KD (NKD) loss and customized soft labels for both target class (image's category) and non-target classes named Universal Self-Knowledge Distillation (USKD). We decompose the KD loss and find the non-target loss from it forces the student's non-target logits to match the teacher's, but the sum of the two non-target logits is different, preventing them from being identical. NKD normalizes the non-target logits to equalize their sum. It can be generally used for KD and self-KD to better use the soft labels for distillation loss. USKD generates customized soft labels for both target and non-target classes without a teacher. It smooths the target logit of the student as the soft target label and uses the rank of the intermediate feature to generate the soft non-target labels with Zipf's law. For KD with teachers, our NKD achieves state-of-the-art performance on CIFAR-100 and ImageNet datasets, boosting the ImageNet Top-1 accuracy of ResNet18 from 69.90% to 71.96% with a ResNet-34 teacher. For self-KD without teachers, USKD is the first self-KD method that can be effectively applied to both CNN and ViT models with negligible additional time and memory cost, resulting in new state-of-the-art results, such as 1.17% and 0.55% accuracy gains on ImageNet for MobileNet and DeiT-Tiny, respectively. Our codes are available at https://github.com/yzd-v/cls_KD.

We decompose the KD loss and find the non-target loss from it forces the student's non-target logits to match the teacher's, but the sum of the two non-target logits is different, preventing them from being identical. NKD normalizes the non-target logits to equalize their sum. It can be generally used for KD and self-KD to better use the soft labels for distillation loss. USKD generates customized soft labels for both target and non-target classes without a teacher. It smooths the target logit of the student as the soft target label and uses the rank of the intermediate feature to generate the soft non-target labels with Zipf's law. For KD with teachers, our NKD achieves state-of-the-art performance on CIFAR-100 and ImageNet datasets, boosting the ImageNet Top-1 accuracy of ResNet18 from 69.90% to 71.96% with a ResNet-34 teacher. For self-KD without teachers, USKD is the first self-KD method that can be effectively applied to both CNN and ViT models with negligible additional time and memory cost, resulting in new state-of-the-art results, such as 1.17% and 0.55% accuracy gains on ImageNet for MobileNet and DeiT-Tiny, respectively. Our codes are available at https://github.com/yzd-v/cls_KD.
abstract_zh: 知识蒸馏（KD）使用教师的预测逻辑作为软标签来指导学生，而自我 KD 不需要真正的教师来要求软标签。这项工作通过将通用 KD 损失分解和重组为归一化 KD (NKD) 损失和目标类（图像类别）和非目标类的定制软标签（称为通用自知识蒸馏（USKD）），统一了这两个任务的表述。我们分解KD损失并从中发现非目标损失迫使学生的非目标logits与老师的相匹配，但是两个非目标logits的总和不同，防止它们相同。 NKD 对非目标 logits 进行标准化以使其总和相等。一般可用于KD和自KD，以更好地利用软标签进行蒸馏损失。 USKD 在没有老师的情况下为目标和非目标类别生成定制的软标签。它将学生的目标logit平滑作为软目标标签，并利用中间特征的等级通过Zipf定律生成软非目标标签。对于教师的 KD，我们的 NKD 在 CIFAR-100 和 ImageNet 数据集上实现了最先进的性能，在 ResNet-34 教师的情况下将 ResNet18 的 ImageNet Top-1 准确率从 69.90% 提高到 71.96%。对于没有教师的自我 KD，USKD 是第一个可以有效应用于 CNN 和 ViT 模型的自我 KD 方法，而额外的时间和内存成本可以忽略不计，从而产生了新的最先进的结果，例如 MobileNet 和 DeiT-Tiny 的 ImageNet 的准确率分别提高了 1.17% 和 0.55%。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/yzd-v/cls_KD 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种统一的知识蒸馏方法，通过归一化损失和自定义软标签，将传统知识蒸馏（KD）与自知识蒸馏（self-KD）结合，提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 传统KD依赖教师模型生成软标签，而self-KD无需教师但效果受限。本文旨在通过统一框架解决两者局限性，提升蒸馏效率与准确性。
AI.method: 分解通用KD损失为归一化KD（NKD）损失和自定义软标签，提出Universal Self-KD（USKD）。NKD归一化非目标类logits以平衡求和，USKD利用Zipf定律生成无教师的软标签，适用于CNN和ViT模型。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-100和ImageNet上取得SOTA结果，ResNet18在ImageNet上Top-1准确率从69.90%提升至71.96%；USKD在MobileNet和DeiT-Tiny上分别提升1.17%和0.55%准确率，且计算开销极小。
AI.conclusion: 本文通过NKD和USKD统一KD与self-KD，显著提升蒸馏效果，为模型压缩提供高效且通用的解决方案，并验证了方法在多种架构上的有效性。

tldr: 本文提出一种统一的知识蒸馏方法，通过归一化损失和自定义软标签，将传统知识蒸馏（KD）与自知识蒸馏（self-KD）结合，提升模型性能。
motivation: 传统KD依赖教师模型生成软标签，而self-KD无需教师但效果受限。本文旨在通过统一框架解决两者局限性，提升蒸馏效率与准确性。
method: 分解通用KD损失为归一化KD（NKD）损失和自定义软标签，提出Universal Self-KD（USKD）。NKD归一化非目标类logits以平衡求和，USKD利用Zipf定律生成无教师的软标签，适用于CNN和ViT模型。
result: 在CIFAR-100和ImageNet上取得SOTA结果，ResNet18在ImageNet上Top-1准确率从69.90%提升至71.96%；USKD在MobileNet和DeiT-Tiny上分别提升1.17%和0.55%准确率，且计算开销极小。
conclusion: 本文通过NKD和USKD统一KD与self-KD，显著提升蒸馏效果，为模型压缩提供高效且通用的解决方案，并验证了方法在多种架构上的有效性。
search_text: From Knowledge Distillation to Self-Knowledge Distillation: A Unified Approach with Normalized Loss and Customized Soft Labels 从知识蒸馏到自我知识蒸馏：具有归一化损失和定制软标签的统一方法 Knowledge Distillation (KD) uses the teacher's prediction logits as soft labels to guide the student, while self-KD does not need a real teacher to require the soft labels. This work unifies the formulations of the two tasks by decomposing and reorganizing the generic KD loss into a Normalized KD (NKD) loss and customized soft labels for both target class (image's category) and non-target classes named Universal Self-Knowledge Distillation (USKD). We decompose the KD loss and find the non-target loss from it forces the student's non-target logits to match the teacher's, but the sum of the two non-target logits is different, preventing them from being identical. NKD normalizes the non-target logits to equalize their sum. It can be generally used for KD and self-KD to better use the soft labels for distillation loss. USKD generates customized soft labels for both target and non-target classes without a teacher. It smooths the target logit of the student as the soft target label and uses the rank of the intermediate feature to generate the soft non-target labels with Zipf's law. For KD with teachers, our NKD achieves state-of-the-art performance on CIFAR-100 and ImageNet datasets, boosting the ImageNet Top-1 accuracy of ResNet18 from 69.90% to 71.96% with a ResNet-34 teacher. For self-KD without teachers, USKD is the first self-KD method that can be effectively applied to both CNN and ViT models with negligible additional time and memory cost, resulting in new state-of-the-art results, such as 1.17% and 0.55% accuracy gains on ImageNet for MobileNet and DeiT-Tiny, respectively. Our codes are available at https://github.com/yzd-v/cls_KD. 知识蒸馏（KD）使用教师的预测逻辑作为软标签来指导学生，而自我 KD 不需要真正的教师来要求软标签。这项工作通过将通用 KD 损失分解和重组为归一化 KD (NKD) 损失和目标类（图像类别）和非目标类的定制软标签（称为通用自知识蒸馏（USKD）），统一了这两个任务的表述。我们分解KD损失并从中发现非目标损失迫使学生的非目标logits与老师的相匹配，但是两个非目标logits的总和不同，防止它们相同。 NKD 对非目标 logits 进行标准化以使其总和相等。一般可用于KD和自KD，以更好地利用软标签进行蒸馏损失。 USKD 在没有老师的情况下为目标和非目标类别生成定制的软标签。它将学生的目标logit平滑作为软目标标签，并利用中间特征的等级通过Zipf定律生成软非目标标签。对于教师的 KD，我们的 NKD 在 CIFAR-100 和 ImageNet 数据集上实现了最先进的性能，在 ResNet-34 教师的情况下将 ResNet18 的 ImageNet Top-1 准确率从 69.90% 提高到 71.96%。对于没有教师的自我 KD，USKD 是第一个可以有效应用于 CNN 和 ViT 模型的自我 KD 方法，而额外的时间和内存成本可以忽略不计，从而产生了新的最先进的结果，例如 MobileNet 和 DeiT-Tiny 的 ImageNet 的准确率分别提高了 1.17% 和 0.55%。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/yzd-v/cls_KD 获取。 本文提出一种统一的知识蒸馏方法，通过归一化损失和自定义软标签，将传统知识蒸馏（KD）与自知识蒸馏（self-KD）结合，提升模型性能。 Zhendong Yang,Ailing Zeng,Zhe Li,Tianke Zhang,Chun Yuan,Yu Li cs.CV

authors_text: Xin Gong,Jintao Peng,Dong Yang,Zhan Shu,Tingwen Huang,Yukang Cui
title: Data-Driven Leader-following Consensus for Nonlinear Multi-Agent Systems against Composite Attacks: A Twins Layer Approach
title_zh: 数据驱动的非线性多代理系统对抗复合攻击的领导者跟随共识：孪生层方法
abstract: This paper studies the leader-following consensuses of uncertain and nonlinear multi-agent systems against composite attacks (CAs), including Denial of Service (DoS) attacks and actuation attacks (AAs). A double-layer control framework is formulated, where a digital twin layer (TL) is added beside the traditional cyber-physical layer (CPL), inspired by the recent Digital Twin technology. Consequently, the resilient control task against CAs can be divided into two parts: One is distributed estimation against DoS attacks on the TL and the other is resilient decentralized tracking control against actuation attacks on the CPL. %The data-driven scheme is used to deal with both model non-linearity and model uncertainty, in which only the input and output data of the system are employed throughout the whole control process. First, a distributed observer based on switching estimation law against DoS is designed on TL. Second, a distributed model free adaptive control (DMFAC) protocol based on attack compensation against AAs is designed on CPL. Moreover, the uniformly ultimately bounded convergence of consensus error of the proposed double-layer DMFAC algorithm is strictly proved. Finally, the simulation verifies the effectiveness of the resilient double-layer control scheme.
summary: This paper studies the leader-following consensuses of uncertain and nonlinear multi-agent systems against composite attacks (CAs), including Denial of Service (DoS) attacks and actuation attacks (AAs). A double-layer control framework is formulated, where a digital twin layer (TL) is added beside the traditional cyber-physical layer (CPL), inspired by the recent Digital Twin technology. Consequently, the resilient control task against CAs can be divided into two parts: One is distributed estimation against DoS attacks on the TL and the other is resilient decentralized tracking control against actuation attacks on the CPL. %The data-driven scheme is used to deal with both model non-linearity and model uncertainty, in which only the input and output data of the system are employed throughout the whole control process. First, a distributed observer based on switching estimation law against DoS is designed on TL. Second, a distributed model free adaptive control (DMFAC) protocol based on attack compensation against AAs is designed on CPL. Moreover, the uniformly ultimately bounded convergence of consensus error of the proposed double-layer DMFAC algorithm is strictly proved. Finally, the simulation verifies the effectiveness of the resilient double-layer control scheme.
abstract_zh: 本文研究了不确定非线性多智能体系统针对复合攻击（CA）的领导者跟随共识，包括拒绝服务（DoS）攻击和驱动攻击（AA）。受最新数字孪生技术的启发，制定了双层控制框架，在传统的网络物理层（CPL）之外添加了数字孪生层（TL）。因此，针对CA的弹性控制任务可以分为两部分：一是针对TL上的DoS攻击的分布式估计，另一部分是针对CPL上的驱动攻击的弹性分散跟踪控制。 %数据驱动方案用于同时处理模型非线性和模型不确定性，整个控制过程仅采用系统的输入和输出数据。首先，在TL上设计了一种基于切换估计律的抗DoS分布式观测器。其次，在CPL上设计了一种基于AA攻击补偿的分布式无模型自适应控制（DMFAC）协议。此外，严格证明了所提出的双层DMFAC算法一致性误差的一致最终有界收敛性。最后，仿真验证了弹性双层控制方案的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在复合攻击（包括拒绝服务攻击和执行攻击）下，不确定非线性多智能体系统的领导者跟随一致性问题，并提出了一种基于双层控制框架的解决方案。
AI.motivation: 现有的控制方法难以有效应对复合攻击对多智能体系统的影响，特别是针对非线性系统和模型不确定性问题缺乏有效的解决方案。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种双层控制框架，包含数字孪生层和传统网络物理层。数字孪生层用于分布式估计以抵御拒绝服务攻击，网络物理层采用基于攻击补偿的分布式模型无关自适应控制协议以抵御执行攻击。
AI.result: 通过严格的数学证明，证明了所提出双层控制算法的一致性误差具有统一最终有界收敛性，并通过仿真验证了该方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的双层控制方法能够有效应对复合攻击，提高了非线性多智能体系统的鲁棒性和一致性性能。

authors_text: Shuhong Lu,Youngwoo Yoon,Andrew Feng
title: Co-Speech Gesture Synthesis using Discrete Gesture Token Learning
title_zh: 使用离散手势令牌学习的协同语音手势合成
comment: 8 pages, 3 figures, 3 tables
comments: 8 pages, 3 figures, 3 tables
abstract: Synthesizing realistic co-speech gestures is an important and yet unsolved problem for creating believable motions that can drive a humanoid robot to interact and communicate with human users. Such capability will improve the impressions of the robots by human users and will find applications in education, training, and medical services. One challenge in learning the co-speech gesture model is that there may be multiple viable gesture motions for the same speech utterance. The deterministic regression methods can not resolve the conflicting samples and may produce over-smoothed or damped motions. We proposed a two-stage model to address this uncertainty issue in gesture synthesis by modeling the gesture segments as discrete latent codes. Our method utilizes RQ-VAE in the first stage to learn a discrete codebook consisting of gesture tokens from training data. In the second stage, a two-level autoregressive transformer model is used to learn the prior distribution of residual codes conditioned on input speech context. Since the inference is formulated as token sampling, multiple gesture sequences could be generated given the same speech input using top-k sampling. The quantitative results and the user study showed the proposed method outperforms the previous methods and is able to generate realistic and diverse gesture motions.
summary: Synthesizing realistic co-speech gestures is an important and yet unsolved problem for creating believable motions that can drive a humanoid robot to interact and communicate with human users. Such capability will improve the impressions of the robots by human users and will find applications in education, training, and medical services. One challenge in learning the co-speech gesture model is that there may be multiple viable gesture motions for the same speech utterance. The deterministic regression methods can not resolve the conflicting samples and may produce over-smoothed or damped motions. We proposed a two-stage model to address this uncertainty issue in gesture synthesis by modeling the gesture segments as discrete latent codes. Our method utilizes RQ-VAE in the first stage to learn a discrete codebook consisting of gesture tokens from training data. In the second stage, a two-level autoregressive transformer model is used to learn the prior distribution of residual codes conditioned on input speech context. Since the inference is formulated as token sampling, multiple gesture sequences could be generated given the same speech input using top-k sampling. The quantitative results and the user study showed the proposed method outperforms the previous methods and is able to generate realistic and diverse gesture motions.
abstract_zh: 合成真实的协同语音手势是创建可信动作的一个重要但尚未解决的问题，这些动作可以驱动人形机器人与人类用户进行交互和交流。这种能力将改善人类用户对机器人的印象，并将在教育、培训和医疗服务中得到应用。学习协同语音手势模型的一个挑战是，同一语音可能有多个可行的手势动作。确定性回归方法无法解决冲突样本，并且可能产生过度平滑或阻尼运动。我们提出了一个两阶段模型，通过将手势片段建模为离散潜在代码来解决手势合成中的不确定性问题。我们的方法在第一阶段利用 RQ-VAE 来学习由训练数据中的手势标记组成的离散码本。在第二阶段，使用二级自回归变换器模型来学习以输入语音上下文为条件的残差代码的先验分布。由于推理被公式化为令牌采样，因此在给定相同语音输入的情况下，可以使用 top-k 采样生成多个手势序列。定量结果和用户研究表明，所提出的方法优于以前的方法，并且能够生成逼真且多样化的手势运动。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于离散手势令牌学习的共语手势合成方法，旨在生成真实且多样的手势以驱动人形机器人进行人机交互。
AI.motivation: 现有确定性回归方法难以处理同一语音输入对应多种合理手势的不确定性，导致生成的手势过于平滑或单调，无法满足实际应用中多样性需求。
AI.method: 采用两阶段模型：第一阶段使用RQ-VAE从训练数据中学习离散手势令牌代码本；第二阶段构建双层自回归Transformer模型，基于语音上下文学习残差代码的先验分布，并通过top-k采样生成多组手势序列。
AI.result: 定量实验和用户研究表明，该方法在生成手势的真实性和多样性指标上优于现有方法，能够有效解决手势合成中的不确定性问题。
AI.conclusion: 通过离散令牌建模和分层Transformer架构，本文提出的框架成功实现了共语手势的可控生成，为机器人交互应用提供了更自然的非语言表达方式。

ai_content_json.method: 采用两阶段模型：第一阶段使用RQ-VAE从训练数据中学习离散手势令牌代码本；第二阶段构建双层自回归Transformer模型，基于语音上下文学习残差代码的先验分布，并通过top-k采样生成多组手势序列。
ai_content_json.result: 定量实验和用户研究表明，该方法在生成手势的真实性和多样性指标上优于现有方法，能够有效解决手势合成中的不确定性问题。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 通过离散令牌建模和分层Transformer架构，本文提出的框架成功实现了共语手势的可控生成，为机器人交互应用提供了更自然的非语言表达方式。

search_text: Co-Speech Gesture Synthesis using Discrete Gesture Token Learning 使用离散手势令牌学习的协同语音手势合成 Synthesizing realistic co-speech gestures is an important and yet unsolved problem for creating believable motions that can drive a humanoid robot to interact and communicate with human users. Such capability will improve the impressions of the robots by human users and will find applications in education, training, and medical services. One challenge in learning the co-speech gesture model is that there may be multiple viable gesture motions for the same speech utterance. The deterministic regression methods can not resolve the conflicting samples and may produce over-smoothed or damped motions. We proposed a two-stage model to address this uncertainty issue in gesture synthesis by modeling the gesture segments as discrete latent codes. Our method utilizes RQ-VAE in the first stage to learn a discrete codebook consisting of gesture tokens from training data. In the second stage, a two-level autoregressive transformer model is used to learn the prior distribution of residual codes conditioned on input speech context. Since the inference is formulated as token sampling, multiple gesture sequences could be generated given the same speech input using top-k sampling. The quantitative results and the user study showed the proposed method outperforms the previous methods and is able to generate realistic and diverse gesture motions. 合成真实的协同语音手势是创建可信动作的一个重要但尚未解决的问题，这些动作可以驱动人形机器人与人类用户进行交互和交流。这种能力将改善人类用户对机器人的印象，并将在教育、培训和医疗服务中得到应用。学习协同语音手势模型的一个挑战是，同一语音可能有多个可行的手势动作。确定性回归方法无法解决冲突样本，并且可能产生过度平滑或阻尼运动。我们提出了一个两阶段模型，通过将手势片段建模为离散潜在代码来解决手势合成中的不确定性问题。我们的方法在第一阶段利用 RQ-VAE 来学习由训练数据中的手势标记组成的离散码本。在第二阶段，使用二级自回归变换器模型来学习以输入语音上下文为条件的残差代码的先验分布。由于推理被公式化为令牌采样，因此在给定相同语音输入的情况下，可以使用 top-k 采样生成多个手势序列。定量结果和用户研究表明，所提出的方法优于以前的方法，并且能够生成逼真且多样化的手势运动。 本文提出了一种基于离散手势令牌学习的共语手势合成方法，旨在生成真实且多样的手势以驱动人形机器人进行人机交互。 Shuhong Lu,Youngwoo Yoon,Andrew Feng cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.RO

authors_text: Tommaso Calò,Luigi De Russis
title: Towards A Visual Programming Tool to Create Deep Learning Models
title_zh: 创建深度学习模型的可视化编程工具
abstract: Deep Learning (DL) developers come from different backgrounds, e.g., medicine, genomics, finance, and computer science. To create a DL model, they must learn and use high-level programming languages (e.g., Python), thus needing to handle related setups and solve programming errors. This paper presents DeepBlocks, a visual programming tool that allows DL developers to design, train, and evaluate models without relying on specific programming languages. DeepBlocks works by building on the typical model structure: a sequence of learnable functions whose arrangement defines the specific characteristics of the model. We derived DeepBlocks' design goals from a 5-participants formative interview, and we validated the first implementation of the tool through a typical use case. Results are promising and show that developers could visually design complex DL architectures.
summary: Deep Learning (DL) developers come from different backgrounds, e.g., medicine, genomics, finance, and computer science. To create a DL model, they must learn and use high-level programming languages (e.g., Python), thus needing to handle related setups and solve programming errors. This paper presents DeepBlocks, a visual programming tool that allows DL developers to design, train, and evaluate models without relying on specific programming languages. DeepBlocks works by building on the typical model structure: a sequence of learnable functions whose arrangement defines the specific characteristics of the model. We derived DeepBlocks' design goals from a 5-participants formative interview, and we validated the first implementation of the tool through a typical use case. Results are promising and show that developers could visually design complex DL architectures.
abstract_zh: 深度学习 (DL) 开发人员来自不同的背景，例如医学、基因组学、金融和计算机科学。为了创建深度学习模型，他们必须学习和使用高级编程语言（例如Python），因此需要处理相关设置并解决编程错误。本文介绍了 DeepBlocks，这是一种可视化编程工具，允许深度学习开发人员在不依赖特定编程语言的情况下设计、训练和评估模型。 DeepBlocks 的工作原理是建立在典型的模型结构之上：一系列可学习的函数，其排列定义了模型的具体特征。我们从 5 名参与者的形成性访谈中得出 DeepBlocks 的设计目标，并通过典型用例验证了该工具的首次实现。结果令人鼓舞，表明开发人员可以直观地设计复杂的深度学习架构。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DeepBlocks，一个无需编程即可设计、训练和评估深度学习模型的可视化编程工具。
AI.motivation: 深度学习开发者背景多样，但传统方法需掌握编程语言及处理技术细节，存在门槛。
AI.method: 基于深度学习模型的典型结构（可学习函数序列），结合5名参与者的访谈需求设计工具，并通过典型用例验证。
AI.result: 实验结果表明开发者可通过可视化方式设计复杂深度学习架构，验证了工具的有效性。
AI.conclusion: DeepBlocks展示了可视化编程工具在降低深度学习使用门槛方面的潜力，未来可进一步优化扩展。

authors_text: Borui Cai,Yong Xiang,Longxiang Gao,Di Wu,He Zhang,Jiong Jin,Tom Luan
title: From Wide to Deep: Dimension Lifting Network for Parameter-efficient Knowledge Graph Embedding
title_zh: 从广到深：用于参数高效知识图嵌入的降维网络
abstract: Knowledge graph embedding (KGE) that maps entities and relations into vector representations is essential for downstream applications. Conventional KGE methods require high-dimensional representations to learn the complex structure of knowledge graph, but lead to oversized model parameters. Recent advances reduce parameters by low-dimensional entity representations, while developing techniques (e.g., knowledge distillation or reinvented representation forms) to compensate for reduced dimension. However, such operations introduce complicated computations and model designs that may not benefit large knowledge graphs. To seek a simple strategy to improve the parameter efficiency of conventional KGE models, we take inspiration from that deeper neural networks require exponentially fewer parameters to achieve expressiveness comparable to wider networks for compositional structures. We view all entity representations as a single-layer embedding network, and conventional KGE methods that adopt high-dimensional entity representations equal widening the embedding network to gain expressiveness. To achieve parameter efficiency, we instead propose a deeper embedding network for entity representations, i.e., a narrow entity embedding layer plus a multi-layer dimension lifting network (LiftNet). Experiments on three public datasets show that by integrating LiftNet, four conventional KGE methods with 16-dimensional representations achieve comparable link prediction accuracy as original models that adopt 512-dimensional representations, saving 68.4% to 96.9% parameters.
summary: Knowledge graph embedding (KGE) that maps entities and relations into vector representations is essential for downstream applications. Conventional KGE methods require high-dimensional representations to learn the complex structure of knowledge graph, but lead to oversized model parameters. Recent advances reduce parameters by low-dimensional entity representations, while developing techniques (e.g., knowledge distillation or reinvented representation forms) to compensate for reduced dimension. However, such operations introduce complicated computations and model designs that may not benefit large knowledge graphs. To seek a simple strategy to improve the parameter efficiency of conventional KGE models, we take inspiration from that deeper neural networks require exponentially fewer parameters to achieve expressiveness comparable to wider networks for compositional structures. We view all entity representations as a single-layer embedding network, and conventional KGE methods that adopt high-dimensional entity representations equal widening the embedding network to gain expressiveness. To achieve parameter efficiency, we instead propose a deeper embedding network for entity representations, i.e., a narrow entity embedding layer plus a multi-layer dimension lifting network (LiftNet). Experiments on three public datasets show that by integrating LiftNet, four conventional KGE methods with 16-dimensional representations achieve comparable link prediction accuracy as original models that adopt 512-dimensional representations, saving 68.4% to 96.9% parameters.
abstract_zh: 将实体和关系映射为向量表示的知识图嵌入（KGE）对于下游应用程序至关重要。传统的KGE方法需要高维表示来学习知识图谱的复杂结构，但会导致模型参数过大。最近的进展通过低维实体表示来减少参数，同时开发技术（例如知识蒸馏或重新发明的表示形式）来补偿减少的维度。然而，此类操作引入了复杂的计算和模型设计，可能对大型知识图无益。为了寻求一种简单的策略来提高传统 KGE 模型的参数效率，我们从更深层次的神经网络需要指数级更少的参数来实现与更广泛的组合结构网络相当的表达能力中获得灵感。我们将所有实体表示视为单层嵌入网络，而采用高维实体表示的传统 KGE 方法等于加宽嵌入网络以获得表达能力。为了实现参数效率，我们提出了一种用于实体表示的更深的嵌入网络，即窄实体嵌入层加上多层维度提升网络（LiftNet）。在三个公共数据集上的实验表明，通过集成LiftNet，四种具有16维表示的传统KGE方法实现了与采用512维表示的原始模型相当的链路预测精度，节省了68.4%至96.9%的参数。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种称为LiftNet的深度嵌入网络，用于提高知识图谱嵌入模型的参数效率。
AI.motivation: 传统知识图谱嵌入方法需要高维表示来学习复杂结构，但导致模型参数过多。虽然最近的方法通过降低实体表示的维度并采用其他技术来补偿，但这些方法引入了复杂的计算和模型设计，可能不适合大规模知识图谱。
AI.method: 受深度神经网络在表达能力上优于宽网络的启发，本文将实体表示视为单层嵌入网络，并提出了一种更深层次的嵌入网络，即窄实体嵌入层加上多层维度提升网络（LiftNet）。
AI.result: 在三个公共数据集上的实验表明，集成LiftNet后，四种传统KGE方法在16维表示下达到了与512维表示相当的链接预测准确性，节省了68.4%至96.9%的参数。
AI.conclusion: LiftNet是一种简单有效的策略，可以在不显著影响性能的情况下显著提高传统KGE模型的参数效率。

We view all entity representations as a single-layer embedding network, and conventional KGE methods that adopt high-dimensional entity representations equal widening the embedding network to gain expressiveness. To achieve parameter efficiency, we instead propose a deeper embedding network for entity representations, i.e., a narrow entity embedding layer plus a multi-layer dimension lifting network (LiftNet). Experiments on three public datasets show that by integrating LiftNet, four conventional KGE methods with 16-dimensional representations achieve comparable link prediction accuracy as original models that adopt 512-dimensional representations, saving 68.4% to 96.9% parameters. 将实体和关系映射为向量表示的知识图嵌入（KGE）对于下游应用程序至关重要。传统的KGE方法需要高维表示来学习知识图谱的复杂结构，但会导致模型参数过大。最近的进展通过低维实体表示来减少参数，同时开发技术（例如知识蒸馏或重新发明的表示形式）来补偿减少的维度。然而，此类操作引入了复杂的计算和模型设计，可能对大型知识图无益。为了寻求一种简单的策略来提高传统 KGE 模型的参数效率，我们从更深层次的神经网络需要指数级更少的参数来实现与更广泛的组合结构网络相当的表达能力中获得灵感。我们将所有实体表示视为单层嵌入网络，而采用高维实体表示的传统 KGE 方法等于加宽嵌入网络以获得表达能力。为了实现参数效率，我们提出了一种用于实体表示的更深的嵌入网络，即窄实体嵌入层加上多层维度提升网络（LiftNet）。在三个公共数据集上的实验表明，通过集成LiftNet，四种具有16维表示的传统KGE方法实现了与采用512维表示的原始模型相当的链路预测精度，节省了68.4%至96.9%的参数。 本文提出了一种称为LiftNet的深度嵌入网络，用于提高知识图谱嵌入模型的参数效率。 Borui Cai,Yong Xiang,Longxiang Gao,Di Wu,He Zhang,Jiong Jin,Tom Luan cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CL

authors_text: Amani Al-shawabka,Philip Pietraski,Sudhir B Pattar,Pedram Johari,Tommaso Melodia
title: SignCRF: Scalable Channel-agnostic Data-driven Radio Authentication System
title_zh: SignCRF：可扩展的与通道无关的数据驱动无线电认证系统
comment: 11 pages, 13 figures, 3 tables
comments: 11 pages, 13 figures, 3 tables
abstract: Radio Frequency Fingerprinting through Deep Learning (RFFDL) is a data-driven IoT authentication technique that leverages the unique hardware-level manufacturing imperfections associated with a particular device to recognize (fingerprint) the device based on variations introduced in the transmitted waveform. The proposed SignCRF is a scalable, channel-agnostic, data-driven radio authentication platform with unmatched precision in fingerprinting wireless devices based on their unique manufacturing impairments and independent of the dynamic channel irregularities caused by mobility. SignCRF consists of (i) a baseline classifier finely trained to authenticate devices with high accuracy and at scale; (ii) an environment translator carefully designed and trained to remove the dynamic channel impact from RF signals while maintaining the radio's specific signature; (iii) a Max-Rule module that selects the highest precision authentication technique between the baseline classifier and the environment translator per radio. We design, train, and validate the performance of SignCRF for multiple technologies in dynamic environments and at scale (100 LoRa and 20 WiFi devices). We demonstrate that SignCRF significantly improves the RFFDL performance by achieving as high as 5x and 8x improvement in correct authentication of WiFi and LoRa devices when compared to the state-of-the-art, respectively.
summary: Radio Frequency Fingerprinting through Deep Learning (RFFDL) is a data-driven IoT authentication technique that leverages the unique hardware-level manufacturing imperfections associated with a particular device to recognize (fingerprint) the device based on variations introduced in the transmitted waveform. The proposed SignCRF is a scalable, channel-agnostic, data-driven radio authentication platform with unmatched precision in fingerprinting wireless devices based on their unique manufacturing impairments and independent of the dynamic channel irregularities caused by mobility. SignCRF consists of (i) a baseline classifier finely trained to authenticate devices with high accuracy and at scale; (ii) an environment translator carefully designed and trained to remove the dynamic channel impact from RF signals while maintaining the radio's specific signature; (iii) a Max-Rule module that selects the highest precision authentication technique between the baseline classifier and the environment translator per radio. We design, train, and validate the performance of SignCRF for multiple technologies in dynamic environments and at scale (100 LoRa and 20 WiFi devices). We demonstrate that SignCRF significantly improves the RFFDL performance by achieving as high as 5x and 8x improvement in correct authentication of WiFi and LoRa devices when compared to the state-of-the-art, respectively.
abstract_zh: 通过深度学习进行射频指纹识别 (RFFDL) 是一种数据驱动的物联网身份验证技术，它利用与特定设备相关的独特硬件级制造缺陷，根据传输波形中引入的变化来识别（指纹）设备。所提出的 SignCRF 是一个可扩展、与信道无关、数据驱动的无线电认证平台，基于其独特的制造缺陷，在指纹识别无线设备方面具有无与伦比的精度，并且不受移动性引起的动态信道不规则性的影响。 SignCRF 包含 (i) 一个经过严格训练的基线分类器，可以高精度、大规模地验证设备； (ii) 环境转换器经过精心设计和培训，可消除射频信号的动态信道影响，同时保持无线电的特定特征； (iii) 最大规则模块，用于在每个无线电的基线分类器和环境转换器之间选择最高精度的身份验证技术。我们针对动态环境和大规模（100 个 LoRa 和 20 个 WiFi 设备）中的多种技术设计、训练和验证 SignCRF 的性能。我们证明，与最先进的技术相比，SignCRF 在 WiFi 和 LoRa 设备的正确身份验证方面分别实现了高达 5 倍和 8 倍的改进，从而显着提高了 RFFDL 性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SignCRF系统，通过深度学习实现无线设备的可扩展、与信道无关的射频指纹认证。
AI.motivation: 现有射频指纹认证技术易受动态信道干扰影响，导致认证精度下降，需开发与信道无关且高精度的认证方案。
AI.method: 设计包含基线分类器（高精度设备认证）、环境转换器（消除信道影响并保留设备特征）及Max-Rule模块（动态选择最优认证方法）的SignCRF框架，并在100个LoRa和20个WiFi设备上验证。
AI.result: SignCRF在WiFi设备认证中较现有技术提升5倍，在LoRa设备中提升8倍，显著提高认证准确率。
AI.conclusion: SignCRF通过分离信道干扰与设备特征，有效提升射频指纹认证性能，为大规模物联网设备认证提供可靠方案。

authors_text: Shrivats Agrawal
title: Are LLMs the Master of All Trades? : Exploring Domain-Agnostic Reasoning Skills of LLMs
title_zh: LLM 是万事通吗？ ：探索法学硕士的领域无关推理技能
abstract: The potential of large language models (LLMs) to reason like humans has been a highly contested topic in Machine Learning communities. However, the reasoning abilities of humans are multifaceted and can be seen in various forms, including analogical, spatial and moral reasoning, among others. This fact raises the question whether LLMs can perform equally well across all these different domains. This research work aims to investigate the performance of LLMs on different reasoning tasks by conducting experiments that directly use or draw inspirations from existing datasets on analogical and spatial reasoning. Additionally, to evaluate the ability of LLMs to reason like human, their performance is evaluted on more open-ended, natural language questions. My findings indicate that LLMs excel at analogical and moral reasoning, yet struggle to perform as proficiently on spatial reasoning tasks. I believe these experiments are crucial for informing the future development of LLMs, particularly in contexts that require diverse reasoning proficiencies. By shedding light on the reasoning abilities of LLMs, this study aims to push forward our understanding of how they can better emulate the cognitive abilities of humans.
summary: The potential of large language models (LLMs) to reason like humans has been a highly contested topic in Machine Learning communities. However, the reasoning abilities of humans are multifaceted and can be seen in various forms, including analogical, spatial and moral reasoning, among others. This fact raises the question whether LLMs can perform equally well across all these different domains. This research work aims to investigate the performance of LLMs on different reasoning tasks by conducting experiments that directly use or draw inspirations from existing datasets on analogical and spatial reasoning. Additionally, to evaluate the ability of LLMs to reason like human, their performance is evaluted on more open-ended, natural language questions. My findings indicate that LLMs excel at analogical and moral reasoning, yet struggle to perform as proficiently on spatial reasoning tasks. I believe these experiments are crucial for informing the future development of LLMs, particularly in contexts that require diverse reasoning proficiencies. By shedding light on the reasoning abilities of LLMs, this study aims to push forward our understanding of how they can better emulate the cognitive abilities of humans.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）像人类一样推理的潜力一直是机器学习社区中备受争议的话题。然而，人类的推理能力是多方面的，可以表现为多种形式，包括类比推理、空间推理和道德推理等。这一事实提出了一个问题：法学硕士是否可以在所有这些不同领域中表现同样出色。这项研究工作旨在通过直接使用现有类比和空间推理数据集或从现有数据集汲取灵感的实验来研究法学硕士在不同推理任务上的表现。此外，为了评估法学硕士像人类一样推理的能力，他们的表现是根据更开放的自然语言问题来评估的。我的研究结果表明，法学硕士擅长类比和道德推理，但在空间推理任务上却难以熟练地表现。我相信这些实验对于法学硕士的未来发展至关重要，特别是在需要多种推理能力的情况下。通过揭示法学硕士的推理能力，这项研究旨在推动我们对他们如何更好地模仿人类认知能力的理解。

AI.tldr: 评估大语言模型在类比、空间和道德推理等不同领域的推理能力，探索其是否具备跨领域的通用推理能力。
AI.motivation: 探讨LLMs是否能在不同领域（如类比、空间、道德推理）表现出与人类相当的推理能力，以推动其在需要多样化推理能力的应用场景中的发展。
AI.method: 通过直接使用或借鉴现有的类比和空间推理数据集，以及在开放式的自然语言问题上进行实验，评估LLMs在不同推理任务中的表现。
AI.result: 研究发现LLMs在类比和道德推理任务中表现优异，但在空间推理任务中表现较弱。
AI.conclusion: 实验结果表明LLMs的推理能力存在领域差异，未来需针对不同推理任务优化模型，以更接近人类的多领域推理能力。

ai_content.tldr: 评估大语言模型在类比、空间和道德推理等不同领域的推理能力，探索其是否具备跨领域的通用推理能力。

authors_text: Vaibhav Garg,Ganning Xu,Munindar P. Singh
title: PACO: Provocation Involving Action, Culture, and Oppression
title_zh: PACO：涉及行动、文化和压迫的挑衅
abstract: In India, people identify with a particular group based on certain attributes such as religion. The same religious groups are often provoked against each other. Previous studies show the role of provocation in increasing tensions between India's two prominent religious groups: Hindus and Muslims. With the advent of the Internet, such provocation also surfaced on social media platforms such as WhatsApp. By leveraging an existing dataset of Indian WhatsApp posts, we identified three categories of provoking sentences against Indian Muslims. Further, we labeled 7,000 sentences for three provocation categories and called this dataset PACO. We leveraged PACO to train a model that can identify provoking sentences from a WhatsApp post. Our best model is fine-tuned RoBERTa and achieved a 0.851 average AUC score over five-fold cross-validation. Automatically identifying provoking sentences could stop provoking text from reaching out to the masses, and can prevent possible discrimination or violence against the target religious group. Further, we studied the provocative speech through a pragmatic lens, by identifying the dialog acts and impoliteness super-strategies used against the religious group.
summary: In India, people identify with a particular group based on certain attributes such as religion. The same religious groups are often provoked against each other. Previous studies show the role of provocation in increasing tensions between India's two prominent religious groups: Hindus and Muslims. With the advent of the Internet, such provocation also surfaced on social media platforms such as WhatsApp. By leveraging an existing dataset of Indian WhatsApp posts, we identified three categories of provoking sentences against Indian Muslims. Further, we labeled 7,000 sentences for three provocation categories and called this dataset PACO. We leveraged PACO to train a model that can identify provoking sentences from a WhatsApp post. Our best model is fine-tuned RoBERTa and achieved a 0.851 average AUC score over five-fold cross-validation. Automatically identifying provoking sentences could stop provoking text from reaching out to the masses, and can prevent possible discrimination or violence against the target religious group. Further, we studied the provocative speech through a pragmatic lens, by identifying the dialog acts and impoliteness super-strategies used against the religious group.
abstract_zh: 在印度，人们根据宗教等某些属性来认同某个特定群体。同一宗教团体经常互相挑衅。此前的研究表明，挑衅在加剧印度两个著名宗教团体：印度教徒和穆斯林之间的紧张关系中发挥了作用。随着互联网的出现，此类挑衅行为也出现在WhatsApp等社交媒体平台上。通过利用印度 WhatsApp 帖子的现有数据集，我们确定了三类针对印度穆斯林的挑衅性句子。此外，我们为三个挑衅类别标记了 7,000 个句子，并将该数据集称为 PACO。我们利用 PACO 训练一个模型，该模型可以识别 WhatsApp 帖子中的挑衅性句子。我们最好的模型是经过微调的 RoBERTa，在五倍交叉验证中获得了 0.851 的平均 AUC 分数。自动识别挑衅性句子可以阻止挑衅性文本传播到大众，并可以防止针对目标宗教团体可能存在的歧视或暴力。此外，我们通过务实的视角研究了挑衅性言论，识别了针对宗教团体的对话行为和不礼貌的超级策略。

AI.tldr: 构建PACO数据集并训练模型自动识别印度社交媒体中针对穆斯林的挑衅言论，以减少宗教冲突。
AI.motivation: 印度社交媒体上的宗教挑衅言论加剧了教派矛盾，需通过自动识别技术预防歧视与暴力事件。
AI.method: 基于印度WhatsApp数据集标注7000条挑衅语句构建PACO数据集，采用微调RoBERTa模型进行分类训练，并通过语用学分析对话行为与不礼貌策略。
AI.result: 最佳模型在五折交叉验证中达到0.851平均AUC值，识别出挑衅言论的对话行为模式及不礼貌策略类型。
AI.conclusion: 自动识别挑衅言论可有效阻断仇恨传播，同时揭示了宗教冲突中语言使用的深层语用特征。

Previous studies show the role of provocation in increasing tensions between India's two prominent religious groups: Hindus and Muslims. authors_text: Shuyin Xia,Xinyu Lin,Guan Wang,De-Gang Chen,Sen Zhao,Guoyin Wang,Jing Liang
title: GBO:AMulti-Granularity Optimization Algorithm via Granular-ball for Continuous Problems
title_zh: GBO：一种针对连续问题的粒球多粒度优化算法
comment: 12 pages, 30 figures
comments: 12 pages, 30 figures
abstract: Optimization problems aim to find the optimal solution, which is becoming increasingly complex and difficult to solve. Traditional evolutionary optimization methods always overlook the granular characteristics of solution space. In the real scenario of numerous optimizations, the solution space is typically partitioned into sub-regions characterized by varying degree distributions. These sub-regions present different granularity characteristics at search potential and difficulty. Considering the granular characteristics of the solution space, the number of coarse-grained regions is smaller than the number of points, so the calculation is more efficient. On the other hand, coarse-grained characteristics are not easily affected by fine-grained sample points, so the calculation is more robust. To this end, this paper proposes a new multi-granularity evolutionary optimization method, namely the Granular-ball Optimization (GBO) algorithm, which characterizes and searches the solution space from coarse to fine. Specifically, using granular-balls instead of traditional points for optimization increases the diversity and robustness of the random search process. At the same time, the search range in different iteration processes is limited by the radius of granular-balls, covering the solution space from large to small. The mechanism of granular-ball splitting is applied to continuously split and evolve the large granular-balls into smaller ones for refining the solution space. Extensive experiments on commonly used benchmarks have shown that GBO outperforms popular and advanced evolutionary algorithms. The code can be found in the supporting materials.

abstract_zh: 优化问题旨在寻找最优解，该问题变得越来越复杂且难以解决。传统的进化优化方法总是忽视解空间的粒度特征。在大量优化的实际场景中，解空间通常被划分为具有不同程度分布特征的子区域。这些子区域在搜索潜力和难度方面呈现出不同的粒度特征。考虑到解空间的粒度特征，粗粒度区域的数量小于点的数量，因此计算效率更高。另一方面，粗粒度的特征不易受到细粒度样本点的影响，因此计算更加鲁棒。为此，本文提出一种新的多粒度进化优化方法，即粒球优化（GBO）算法，该算法从粗到细地表征和搜索解空间。具体来说，使用粒状球代替传统的点进行优化可以增加随机搜索过程的多样性和鲁棒性。同时，不同迭代过程中的搜索范围受到粒球半径的限制，从大到小覆盖解空间。采用粒球分裂机制，将大粒球不断分裂并演化成更小的粒球，以细化解空间。对常用基准的大量实验表明，GBO 的性能优于流行且先进的进化算法。该代码可以在支持材料中找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于粒球的多粒度优化算法（GBO），通过粒球机制从粗到细搜索解空间，解决连续优化问题。
AI.motivation: 传统进化算法忽视解空间的粒度特征，导致搜索效率与鲁棒性不足，需通过多粒度划分提升优化性能。
AI.method: 使用粒球替代传统点进行搜索，结合粒球分裂机制逐步细化解空间，并通过粒球半径控制搜索范围。
AI.result: 在常用基准测试中，GBO算法表现优于主流先进进化算法，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: GBO算法通过多粒度粒球机制提升了优化效率与鲁棒性，为复杂连续问题提供了新思路。

authors_text: Tirtha Chanda,Katja Hauser,Sarah Hobelsberger,Tabea-Clara Bucher,Carina Nogueira Garcia,Christoph Wies,Harald Kittler,Philipp Tschandl,Cristian Navarrete-Dechent,Sebastian Podlipnik,Emmanouil Chousakos,Iva Crnaric,Jovana Majstorovic,Linda Alhajwan,Tanya Foreman,Sandra Peternel,Sergei Sarap,İrem Özdemir,Raymond L. Barnhill,Mar Llamas Velasco,Gabriela Poch,Sören Korsing,Wiebke Sondermann,Frank Friedrich Gellrich,Markus V. Heppt, et al. (10 additional authors not shown)
title: Dermatologist-like explainable AI enhances trust and confidence in diagnosing melanoma
title_zh: 类似皮肤科医生的可解释人工智能增强了诊断黑色素瘤的信任和信心
abstract: Although artificial intelligence (AI) systems have been shown to improve the accuracy of initial melanoma diagnosis, the lack of transparency in how these systems identify melanoma poses severe obstacles to user acceptance. Explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods can help to increase transparency, but most XAI methods are unable to produce precisely located domain-specific explanations, making the explanations difficult to interpret. Moreover, the impact of XAI methods on dermatologists has not yet been evaluated. Extending on two existing classifiers, we developed an XAI system that produces text and region based explanations that are easily interpretable by dermatologists alongside its differential diagnoses of melanomas and nevi. To evaluate this system, we conducted a three-part reader study to assess its impact on clinicians' diagnostic accuracy, confidence, and trust in the XAI-support. We showed that our XAI's explanations were highly aligned with clinicians' explanations and that both the clinicians' trust in the support system and their confidence in their diagnoses were significantly increased when using our XAI compared to using a conventional AI system. The clinicians' diagnostic accuracy was numerically, albeit not significantly, increased. This work demonstrates that clinicians are willing to adopt such an XAI system, motivating their future use in the clinic.
summary: Although artificial intelligence (AI) systems have been shown to improve the accuracy of initial melanoma diagnosis, the lack of transparency in how these systems identify melanoma poses severe obstacles to user acceptance. Explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods can help to increase transparency, but most XAI methods are unable to produce precisely located domain-specific explanations, making the explanations difficult to interpret. Moreover, the impact of XAI methods on dermatologists has not yet been evaluated. Extending on two existing classifiers, we developed an XAI system that produces text and region based explanations that are easily interpretable by dermatologists alongside its differential diagnoses of melanomas and nevi. To evaluate this system, we conducted a three-part reader study to assess its impact on clinicians' diagnostic accuracy, confidence, and trust in the XAI-support. We showed that our XAI's explanations were highly aligned with clinicians' explanations and that both the clinicians' trust in the support system and their confidence in their diagnoses were significantly increased when using our XAI compared to using a conventional AI system. The clinicians' diagnostic accuracy was numerically, albeit not significantly, increased. This work demonstrates that clinicians are willing to adopt such an XAI system, motivating their future use in the clinic.
abstract_zh: 尽管人工智能（AI）系统已被证明可以提高黑色素瘤初始诊断的准确性，但这些系统识别黑色素瘤的方式缺乏透明度，给用户接受度带来了严重障碍。可解释的人工智能 (XAI) 方法有助于提高透明度，但大多数 XAI 方法无法生成精确定位的特定领域的解释，使得解释难以解释。此外，XAI 方法对皮肤科医生的影响尚未得到评估。在两个现有分类器的基础上，我们开发了一个 XAI 系统，该系统可以生成基于文本和区域的解释，这些解释很容易被皮肤科医生解释，同时还能对黑色素瘤和痣进行鉴别诊断。为了评估该系统，我们进行了一项由三部分组成的读者研究，以评估其对临床医生诊断准确性、信心和对 XAI 支持的信任的影响。我们表明，我们的 XAI 的解释与临床医生的解释高度一致，并且与使用传统 AI 系统相比，使用我们的 XAI 时，临床医生对支持系统的信任以及对诊断的信心都显着增加。临床医生的诊断准确性在数字上得到了提高，尽管没有显着提高。这项工作表明临床医生愿意采用这样的 XAI 系统，从而激励其未来在临床中的使用。

AI.tldr: 开发一种可解释性人工智能（XAI）系统，用于提高皮肤科医生对黑色素瘤诊断的置信度和信任度。
AI.motivation: 现有AI系统在黑色素瘤诊断中准确性高但缺乏透明度，导致用户接受度低；现有XAI方法难以生成精准的医学领域解释，且其对皮肤科医生的影响未被评估。
AI.method: 基于两种现有分类器开发XAI系统，生成文本和区域解释以辅助诊断，并通过三部分读者研究评估其对临床医生诊断准确性、置信度和信任度的影响。
AI.result: XAI的解释与临床医生高度一致，使用XAI时医生的信任度和诊断置信度显著提升，诊断准确性略有提高但未达统计学意义。
AI.conclusion: 临床医生愿意采用该XAI系统，为未来临床应用提供了依据。

search_text: Dermatologist-like explainable AI enhances trust and confidence in diagnosing melanoma 类似皮肤科医生的可解释人工智能增强了诊断黑色素瘤的信任和信心 Although artificial intelligence (AI) systems have been shown to improve the accuracy of initial melanoma diagnosis, the lack of transparency in how these systems identify melanoma poses severe obstacles to user acceptance. Explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods can help to increase transparency, but most XAI methods are unable to produce precisely located domain-specific explanations, making the explanations difficult to interpret. Moreover, the impact of XAI methods on dermatologists has not yet been evaluated. Extending on two existing classifiers, we developed an XAI system that produces text and region based explanations that are easily interpretable by dermatologists alongside its differential diagnoses of melanomas and nevi. To evaluate this system, we conducted a three-part reader study to assess its impact on clinicians' diagnostic accuracy, confidence, and trust in the XAI-support. We showed that our XAI's explanations were highly aligned with clinicians' explanations and that both the clinicians' trust in the support system and their confidence in their diagnoses were significantly increased when using our XAI compared to using a conventional AI system. The clinicians' diagnostic accuracy was numerically, albeit not significantly, increased. This work demonstrates that clinicians are willing to adopt such an XAI system, motivating their future use in the clinic. 尽管人工智能（AI）系统已被证明可以提高黑色素瘤初始诊断的准确性，但这些系统识别黑色素瘤的方式缺乏透明度，给用户接受度带来了严重障碍。可解释的人工智能 (XAI) 方法有助于提高透明度，但大多数 XAI 方法无法生成精确定位的特定领域的解释，使得解释难以解释。此外，XAI 方法对皮肤科医生的影响尚未得到评估。在两个现有分类器的基础上，我们开发了一个 XAI 系统，该系统可以生成基于文本和区域的解释，这些解释很容易被皮肤科医生解释，同时还能对黑色素瘤和痣进行鉴别诊断。为了评估该系统，我们进行了一项由三部分组成的读者研究，以评估其对临床医生诊断准确性、信心和对 XAI 支持的信任的影响。我们表明，我们的 XAI 的解释与临床医生的解释高度一致，并且与使用传统 AI 系统相比，使用我们的 XAI 时，临床医生对支持系统的信任以及对诊断的信心都显着增加。临床医生的诊断准确性在数字上得到了提高，尽管没有显着提高。这项工作表明临床医生愿意采用这样的 XAI 系统，从而激励其未来在临床中的使用。 开发一种可解释性人工智能（XAI）系统，用于提高皮肤科医生对黑色素瘤诊断的置信度和信任度。 Tirtha Chanda,Katja Hauser,Sarah Hobelsberger,Tabea-Clara Bucher,Carina Nogueira Garcia,Christoph Wies,Harald Kittler,Philipp Tschandl,Cristian Navarrete-Dechent,Sebastian Podlipnik,Emmanouil Chousakos,Iva Crnaric,Jovana Majstorovic,Linda Alhajwan,Tanya Foreman,Sandra Peternel,Sergei Sarap,İrem Özdemir,Raymond L.
