search_text: Q-HyViT: Post-Training Quantization of Hybrid Vision Transformers with Bridge Block Reconstruction for IoT Systems Q-HyViT：混合视觉变压器的训练后量化与物联网系统的桥块重建 Recently, vision transformers (ViTs) have superseded convolutional neural networks in numerous applications, including classification, detection, and segmentation. However, the high computational requirements of ViTs hinder their widespread implementation. To address this issue, researchers have proposed efficient hybrid transformer architectures that combine convolutional and transformer layers with optimized attention computation of linear complexity. Additionally, post-training quantization has been proposed as a means of mitigating computational demands. For mobile devices, achieving optimal acceleration for ViTs necessitates the strategic integration of quantization techniques and efficient hybrid transformer structures. However, no prior investigation has applied quantization to efficient hybrid transformers. In this paper, we discover that applying existing post-training quantization (PTQ) methods for ViTs to efficient hybrid transformers leads to a drastic accuracy drop, attributed to the four following challenges: (i) highly dynamic ranges, (ii) zero-point overflow, (iii) diverse normalization, and (iv) limited model parameters ($<$5M). To overcome these challenges, we propose a new post-training quantization method, which is the first to quantize efficient hybrid ViTs (MobileViTv1, MobileViTv2, Mobile-Former, EfficientFormerV1, EfficientFormerV2). We achieve a significant improvement of 17.73% for 8-bit and 29.75% for 6-bit on average, respectively, compared with existing PTQ methods (EasyQuant, FQ-ViT, PTQ4ViT, and RepQ-ViT)}. We plan to release our code at https://gitlab.com/ones-ai/q-hyvit. 最近，视觉变换器 (ViT) 在许多应用中取代了卷积神经网络，包括分类、检测和分割。然而，ViT 的高计算要求阻碍了其广泛实施。为了解决这个问题，研究人员提出了高效的混合 Transformer 架构，它将卷积层和 Transformer 层与线性复杂度的优化注意力计算相结合。此外，训练后量化已被提议作为减轻计算需求的一种手段。对于移动设备，实现 ViT 的最佳加速需要量化技术和高效混合变压器结构的战略集成。然而，之前没有研究将量化应用于高效混合变压器。在本文中，我们发现将现有的 ViT 训练后量化 (PTQ) 方法应用于高效混合变压器会导致精度急剧下降，这归因于以下四个挑战：(i) 高动态范围，(ii) 零点溢出，(iii) 多样化归一化，以及 (iv) 有限的模型参数（$<500 万美元）。为了克服这些挑战，我们提出了一种新的训练后量化方法，该方法是第一个量化高效混合 ViT（MobileViTv1、MobileViTv2、Mobile-Former、EfficientFormerV1、EfficientFormerV2）的方法。与现有的 PTQ 方法（EasyQuant、FQ-ViT、PTQ4ViT 和 RepQ-ViT）相比，我们在 8 位和 6 位方面平均分别实现了 17.73% 和 29.75% 的显着改进}。我们计划在 https://gitlab.com/ones-ai/q-hyvit 发布我们的代码。 本文提出Q-HyViT方法，通过桥接块重建技术对混合视觉Transformer进行后训练量化，以提升其在物联网系统中的部署效率。 Jemin Lee,Yongin Kwon,Sihyeong Park,Misun Yu,Jeman Park,Hwanjun Song cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Patrick Godau,Piotr Kalinowski,Evangelia Christodoulou,Annika Reinke,Minu Tizabi,Luciana Ferrer,Paul Jäger,Lena Maier-Hein
title: Deployment of Image Analysis Algorithms under Prevalence Shifts
title_zh: 患病率变化下图像分析算法的部署
abstract: Domain gaps are among the most relevant roadblocks in the clinical translation of machine learning (ML)-based solutions for medical image analysis. While current research focuses on new training paradigms and network architectures, little attention is given to the specific effect of prevalence shifts on an algorithm deployed in practice. Such discrepancies between class frequencies in the data used for a method's development/validation and that in its deployment environment(s) are of great importance, for example in the context of artificial intelligence (AI) democratization, as disease prevalences may vary widely across time and location. Our contribution is twofold. First, we empirically demonstrate the potentially severe consequences of missing prevalence handling by analyzing (i) the extent of miscalibration, (ii) the deviation of the decision threshold from the optimum, and (iii) the ability of validation metrics to reflect neural network performance on the deployment population as a function of the discrepancy between development and deployment prevalence. Second, we propose a workflow for prevalence-aware image classification that uses estimated deployment prevalences to adjust a trained classifier to a new environment, without requiring additional annotated deployment data. Comprehensive experiments based on a diverse set of 30 medical classification tasks showcase the benefit of the proposed workflow in generating better classifier decisions and more reliable performance estimates compared to current practice.
summary: Domain gaps are among the most relevant roadblocks in the clinical translation of machine learning (ML)-based solutions for medical image analysis. While current research focuses on new training paradigms and network architectures, little attention is given to the specific effect of prevalence shifts on an algorithm deployed in practice. Such discrepancies between class frequencies in the data used for a method's development/validation and that in its deployment environment(s) are of great importance, for example in the context of artificial intelligence (AI) democratization, as disease prevalences may vary widely across time and location. Our contribution is twofold. First, we empirically demonstrate the potentially severe consequences of missing prevalence handling by analyzing (i) the extent of miscalibration, (ii) the deviation of the decision threshold from the optimum, and (iii) the ability of validation metrics to reflect neural network performance on the deployment population as a function of the discrepancy between development and deployment prevalence. Second, we propose a workflow for prevalence-aware image classification that uses estimated deployment prevalences to adjust a trained classifier to a new environment, without requiring additional annotated deployment data. Comprehensive experiments based on a diverse set of 30 medical classification tasks showcase the benefit of the proposed workflow in generating better classifier decisions and more reliable performance estimates compared to current practice.
abstract_zh: 领域差距是基于机器学习 (ML) 的医学图像分析解决方案临床转化中最相关的障碍之一。虽然当前的研究重点是新的训练范式和网络架构，但很少关注流行度变化对实践中部署的算法的具体影响。用于方法开发/验证的数据中的类频率与其部署环境中的类频率之间的这种差异非常重要，例如在人工智能（AI）民主化的背景下，因为疾病流行率可能因时间和地点的不同而有很大差异。我们的贡献是双重的。首先，我们通过分析（i）错误校准的程度，（ii）决策阈值与最佳阈值的偏差，以及（iii）验证指标反映神经网络在部署群体上的性能作为开发和部署流行率之间差异的函数的能力，以经验证明缺失流行率处理的潜在严重后果。其次，我们提出了一种流行度感知图像分类的工作流程，该工作流使用估计的部署流行度将经过训练的分类器调整到新环境，而不需要额外的带注释的部署数据。基于 30 个不同的医学分类任务的综合实验展示了所提出的工作流程在生成更好的分类器决策和与当前实践相比更可靠的性能估计方面的优势。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了医学图像分析中机器学习算法在部署环境下的流行度变化问题，并提出了一种无需额外标注数据的流行度自适应分类方法。
AI.motivation: 当前医学图像分析的机器学习模型在部署时面临领域差异的挑战，而流行度变化（即训练数据与实际部署数据的类别分布差异）对模型性能的影响尚未得到充分重视，尤其在AI普及化背景下这一问题更加突出。
AI.method: 首先通过实验证明忽略流行度调整的严重后果，包括校准误差、决策阈值偏移和验证指标失真；其次提出基于部署环境预估流行度的分类器自适应工作流，通过调整分类器参数实现跨环境部署。
AI.result: 在30种医学分类任务上的实验表明，该方法能生成更优的分类决策并提供更可靠的性能评估，相比现有方法具有显著优势。
AI.conclusion: 流行度变化是影响医学AI部署效果的关键因素，本文提出的流行度自适应方法可有效提升模型在真实场景中的鲁棒性和可靠性。

authors_text: Yufei Ye,Xueting Li,Abhinav Gupta,Shalini De Mello,Stan Birchfield,Jiaming Song,Shubham Tulsiani,Sifei Liu
title: Affordance Diffusion: Synthesizing Hand-Object Interactions
title_zh: 可供性扩散：合成手-物体交互
comment: accepted to CVPR22, change fig 2 from .pdf to .jpg for adobe compatibility
comments: accepted to CVPR22, change fig 2 from .pdf to .jpg for adobe compatibility
abstract: Recent successes in image synthesis are powered by large-scale diffusion models. However, most methods are currently limited to either text- or image-conditioned generation for synthesizing an entire image, texture transfer or inserting objects into a user-specified region. In contrast, in this work we focus on synthesizing complex interactions (ie, an articulated hand) with a given object. Given an RGB image of an object, we aim to hallucinate plausible images of a human hand interacting with it. We propose a two-step generative approach: a LayoutNet that samples an articulation-agnostic hand-object-interaction layout, and a ContentNet that synthesizes images of a hand grasping the object given the predicted layout. Both are built on top of a large-scale pretrained diffusion model to make use of its latent representation. Compared to baselines, the proposed method is shown to generalize better to novel objects and perform surprisingly well on out-of-distribution in-the-wild scenes of portable-sized objects. The resulting system allows us to predict descriptive affordance information, such as hand articulation and approaching orientation. Project page: https://judyye.github.io/affordiffusion-www

Compared to baselines, the proposed method is shown to generalize better to novel objects and perform surprisingly well on out-of-distribution in-the-wild scenes of portable-sized objects. The resulting system allows us to predict descriptive affordance information, such as hand articulation and approaching orientation. Project page: https://judyye.github.io/affordiffusion-www
abstract_zh: 图像合成领域的最新成功得益于大规模扩散模型。然而，大多数方法目前仅限于文本或图像条件生成，用于合成整个图像、纹理传输或将对象插入用户指定的区域。相比之下，在这项工作中，我们专注于合成与给定对象的复杂交互（即，铰接的手）。给定一个物体的 RGB 图像，我们的目标是产生人手与其交互的可信图像。我们提出了一种两步生成方法：一个对与关节无关的手-对象交互布局进行采样的 LayoutNet，以及一个在给定预测布局的情况下合成抓握对象的手的图像的 ContentNet。两者都建立在大规模预训练扩散模型之上，以利用其潜在表示。与基线相比，所提出的方法可以更好地推广到新颖的物体，并且在便携式大小物体的野外分布外场景上表现得令人惊讶。由此产生的系统使我们能够预测描述性可供性信息，例如手部清晰度和接近方向。项目页面：https://judyye.github.io/affordiffusion-www

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Affordance Diffusion的方法，通过给定物体的RGB图像合成人类手部与物体交互的合理图像。
AI.motivation: 现有图像生成方法难以合成复杂的手部-物体交互场景，尤其缺乏对可操作性（affordance）信息的建模能力。
AI.method: 采用两阶段生成框架：LayoutNet生成手部-物体布局，ContentNet基于布局合成具体图像，均建立在预训练扩散模型基础上。
AI.result: 在新型物体和真实场景中表现出色，能预测手部关节角度和接近方向等可操作性信息。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了手部-物体交互合成难题，为理解物体功能提供了新的生成式工具。

search_text: Affordance Diffusion: Synthesizing Hand-Object Interactions 可供性扩散：合成手-物体交互 Recent successes in image synthesis are powered by large-scale diffusion models. However, most methods are currently limited to either text- or image-conditioned generation for synthesizing an entire image, texture transfer or inserting objects into a user-specified region. In contrast, in this work we focus on synthesizing complex interactions (ie, an articulated hand) with a given object. Given an RGB image of an object, we aim to hallucinate plausible images of a human hand interacting with it. We propose a two-step generative approach: a LayoutNet that samples an articulation-agnostic hand-object-interaction layout, and a ContentNet that synthesizes images of a hand grasping the object given the predicted layout. Both are built on top of a large-scale pretrained diffusion model to make use of its latent representation. Compared to baselines, the proposed method is shown to generalize better to novel objects and perform surprisingly well on out-of-distribution in-the-wild scenes of portable-sized objects. The resulting system allows us to predict descriptive affordance information, such as hand articulation and approaching orientation. authors_text: Chaoda Zheng,Xu Yan,Haiming Zhang,Baoyuan Wang,Shenghui Cheng,Shuguang Cui,Zhen Li
title: An Effective Motion-Centric Paradigm for 3D Single Object Tracking in Point Clouds
title_zh: 点云中 3D 单对象跟踪的有效的以运动为中心的范式
comment: Accepted version of the journal extension of M^2-Track. Accepted by TPAMI. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2203.01730
comments: Accepted version of the journal extension of M^2-Track. Accepted by TPAMI. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2203.01730
abstract: 3D single object tracking in LiDAR point clouds (LiDAR SOT) plays a crucial role in autonomous driving. Current approaches all follow the Siamese paradigm based on appearance matching. However, LiDAR point clouds are usually textureless and incomplete, which hinders effective appearance matching. Besides, previous methods greatly overlook the critical motion clues among targets. In this work, beyond 3D Siamese tracking, we introduce a motion-centric paradigm to handle LiDAR SOT from a new perspective. Following this paradigm, we propose a matching-free two-stage tracker M^2-Track. At the 1st-stage, M^2-Track localizes the target within successive frames via motion transformation. Then it refines the target box through motion-assisted shape completion at the 2nd-stage. Due to the motion-centric nature, our method shows its impressive generalizability with limited training labels and provides good differentiability for end-to-end cycle training. This inspires us to explore semi-supervised LiDAR SOT by incorporating a pseudo-label-based motion augmentation and a self-supervised loss term. Under the fully-supervised setting, extensive experiments confirm that M^2-Track significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-arts on three large-scale datasets while running at 57FPS (~3%, ~11% and ~22% precision gains on KITTI, NuScenes, and Waymo Open Dataset respectively). While under the semi-supervised setting, our method performs on par with or even surpasses its fully-supervised counterpart using fewer than half of the labels from KITTI. Further analysis verifies each component's effectiveness and shows the motion-centric paradigm's promising potential for auto-labeling and unsupervised domain adaptation.

However, LiDAR point clouds are usually textureless and incomplete, which hinders effective appearance matching. Besides, previous methods greatly overlook the critical motion clues among targets. In this work, beyond 3D Siamese tracking, we introduce a motion-centric paradigm to handle LiDAR SOT from a new perspective. Following this paradigm, we propose a matching-free two-stage tracker M^2-Track. At the 1st-stage, M^2-Track localizes the target within successive frames via motion transformation. Then it refines the target box through motion-assisted shape completion at the 2nd-stage. Due to the motion-centric nature, our method shows its impressive generalizability with limited training labels and provides good differentiability for end-to-end cycle training. This inspires us to explore semi-supervised LiDAR SOT by incorporating a pseudo-label-based motion augmentation and a self-supervised loss term. Under the fully-supervised setting, extensive experiments confirm that M^2-Track significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-arts on three large-scale datasets while running at 57FPS (~3%, ~11% and ~22% precision gains on KITTI, NuScenes, and Waymo Open Dataset respectively). While under the semi-supervised setting, our method performs on par with or even surpasses its fully-supervised counterpart using fewer than half of the labels from KITTI. Further analysis verifies each component's effectiveness and shows the motion-centric paradigm's promising potential for auto-labeling and unsupervised domain adaptation.
abstract_zh: LiDAR 点云 (LiDAR SOT) 中的 3D 单目标跟踪在自动驾驶中发挥着至关重要的作用。当前的方法都遵循基于外观匹配的暹罗范例。然而，激光雷达点云通常无纹理且不完整，这阻碍了有效的外观匹配。此外，以前的方法极大地忽略了目标之间的关键运动线索。在这项工作中，除了 3D 连体跟踪之外，我们还引入了一种以运动为中心的范例，从新的角度处理 LiDAR SOT。遵循这个范式，我们提出了一种无匹配的两级跟踪器 M^2-Track。在第一阶段，M^2-Track 通过运动变换将目标定位在连续帧内。然后，它在第二阶段通过运动辅助形状完成来细化目标框。由于以运动为中心的性质，我们的方法在有限的训练标签下显示出令人印象深刻的通用性，并为端到端循环训练提供了良好的可区分性。这激励我们通过结合基于伪标签的运动增强和自监督损失项来探索半监督 LiDAR SOT。在完全监督的设置下，大量实验证实，M^2-Track 在以 57FPS 运行时，在三个大型数据集上的性能显着优于之前的最先进技术（KITTI、NuScenes 和 Waymo 开放数据集的精度分别提高了约 3%、约 11% 和约 22%）。在半监督设置下，我们的方法使用不到一半的 KITTI 标签，其性能与完全监督的方法相当甚至超过了完全监督的方法。进一步的分析验证了每个组件的有效性，并显示了以运动为中心的范式在自动标记和无监督域适应方面的巨大潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于运动信息的3D单目标跟踪新范式M^2-Track，用于解决LiDAR点云中因纹理缺失和运动线索被忽视导致的跟踪难题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖外观匹配但LiDAR点云缺乏纹理且不完整，同时未充分利用目标间的运动线索，导致跟踪效果受限。
AI.method: 设计两阶段运动感知跟踪器：第一阶段通过运动变换定位目标，第二阶段利用运动辅助形状补全优化目标框；结合伪标签运动增强与自监督损失实现半监督学习。
AI.result: 在KITTI、NuScenes和Waymo数据集上分别提升约3%/11%/22%精度，半监督设置下使用不足一半标注量性能持平或超越全监督方法。
AI.conclusion: 运动中心范式显著提升跟踪性能，验证了其在自动标注和域适应中的潜力，为LiDAR SOT提供新思路。

search_text: An Effective Motion-Centric Paradigm for 3D Single Object Tracking in Point Clouds 点云中 3D 单对象跟踪的有效的以运动为中心的范式 3D single object tracking in LiDAR point clouds (LiDAR SOT) plays a crucial role in autonomous driving. Current approaches all follow the Siamese paradigm based on appearance matching. However, LiDAR point clouds are usually textureless and incomplete, which hinders effective appearance matching. Besides, previous methods greatly overlook the critical motion clues among targets. In this work, beyond 3D Siamese tracking, we introduce a motion-centric paradigm to handle LiDAR SOT from a new perspective. Following this paradigm, we propose a matching-free two-stage tracker M^2-Track. At the 1st-stage, M^2-Track localizes the target within successive frames via motion transformation. Then it refines the target box through motion-assisted shape completion at the 2nd-stage. Due to the motion-centric nature, our method shows its impressive generalizability with limited training labels and provides good differentiability for end-to-end cycle training. This inspires us to explore semi-supervised LiDAR SOT by incorporating a pseudo-label-based motion augmentation and a self-supervised loss term. Under the fully-supervised setting, extensive experiments confirm that M^2-Track significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-arts on three large-scale datasets while running at 57FPS (~3%, ~11% and ~22% precision gains on KITTI, NuScenes, and Waymo Open Dataset respectively). While under the semi-supervised setting, our method performs on par with or even surpasses its fully-supervised counterpart using fewer than half of the labels from KITTI. Further analysis verifies each component's effectiveness and shows the motion-centric paradigm's promising potential for auto-labeling and unsupervised domain adaptation. authors_text: Elliot Vincent,Jean Ponce,Mathieu Aubry
title: Pixel-wise Agricultural Image Time Series Classification: Comparisons and a Deformable Prototype-based Approach
title_zh: 逐像素农业图像时间序列分类：比较和基于可变形原型的方法
comment: Revised version. Added references and baselines. Corrected typos. Added discussion section and Appendix A, B and C
comments: Revised version. Added references and baselines. Corrected typos. Added discussion section and Appendix A, B and C
abstract: Improvements in Earth observation by satellites allow for imagery of ever higher temporal and spatial resolution. Leveraging this data for agricultural monitoring is key for addressing environmental and economic challenges. Current methods for crop segmentation using temporal data either rely on annotated data or are heavily engineered to compensate the lack of supervision. In this paper, we present and compare datasets and methods for both supervised and unsupervised pixel-wise segmentation of satellite image time series (SITS). We also introduce an approach to add invariance to spectral deformations and temporal shifts to classical prototype-based methods such as K-means and Nearest Centroid Classifier (NCC). We study different levels of supervision and show this simple and highly interpretable method achieves the best performance in the low data regime and significantly improves the state of the art for unsupervised classification of agricultural time series on four recent SITS datasets.
summary: Improvements in Earth observation by satellites allow for imagery of ever higher temporal and spatial resolution. Leveraging this data for agricultural monitoring is key for addressing environmental and economic challenges. Current methods for crop segmentation using temporal data either rely on annotated data or are heavily engineered to compensate the lack of supervision. In this paper, we present and compare datasets and methods for both supervised and unsupervised pixel-wise segmentation of satellite image time series (SITS). We also introduce an approach to add invariance to spectral deformations and temporal shifts to classical prototype-based methods such as K-means and Nearest Centroid Classifier (NCC). We study different levels of supervision and show this simple and highly interpretable method achieves the best performance in the low data regime and significantly improves the state of the art for unsupervised classification of agricultural time series on four recent SITS datasets.
abstract_zh: 卫星地球观测的改进使得图像具有更高的时间和空间分辨率。利用这些数据进行农业监测是应对环境和经济挑战的关键。当前使用时间数据进行作物分割的方法要么依赖于注释数据，要么经过精心设计以弥补监督的缺乏。在本文中，我们提出并比较了卫星图像时间序列（SITS）的有监督和无监督像素级分割的数据集和方法。我们还介绍了一种为经典的基于原型的方法（例如 K 均值和最近质心分类器 (NCC)）添加光谱变形和时间平移的不变性的方法。我们研究了不同级别的监督，并表明这种简单且高度可解释的方法在低数据范围内实现了最佳性能，并显着提高了最近四个 SITS 数据集上农业时间序列无监督分类的最新技术水平。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种可变形的基于原型的像素级农业卫星影像时间序列分类方法，并比较了不同监督水平下的数据集和方法。
AI.motivation: 现有农业遥感分类方法依赖标注数据或复杂工程化设计，难以适应数据稀缺和光谱/时间变化带来的挑战。
AI.method: 通过改进经典原型方法（如K-means和最近质心分类器），引入对光谱形变和时间偏移的不变性，实现低数据量下的鲁棒分类。
AI.result: 在四个最新卫星影像数据集上，该方法在低数据场景下表现最佳，显著提升农业时间序列的无监督分类效果。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法具有高可解释性，在数据有限和无监督场景下为农业遥感分类提供了有效解决方案。

search_text: Pixel-wise Agricultural Image Time Series Classification: Comparisons and a Deformable Prototype-based Approach 逐像素农业图像时间序列分类：比较和基于可变形原型的方法 Improvements in Earth observation by satellites allow for imagery of ever higher temporal and spatial resolution. Leveraging this data for agricultural monitoring is key for addressing environmental and economic challenges. Current methods for crop segmentation using temporal data either rely on annotated data or are heavily engineered to compensate the lack of supervision. In this paper, we present and compare datasets and methods for both supervised and unsupervised pixel-wise segmentation of satellite image time series (SITS). We also introduce an approach to add invariance to spectral deformations and temporal shifts to classical prototype-based methods such as K-means and Nearest Centroid Classifier (NCC). We study different levels of supervision and show this simple and highly interpretable method achieves the best performance in the low data regime and significantly improves the state of the art for unsupervised classification of agricultural time series on four recent SITS datasets. 卫星地球观测的改进使得图像具有更高的时间和空间分辨率。利用这些数据进行农业监测是应对环境和经济挑战的关键。当前使用时间数据进行作物分割的方法要么依赖于注释数据，要么经过精心设计以弥补监督的缺乏。在本文中，我们提出并比较了卫星图像时间序列（SITS）的有监督和无监督像素级分割的数据集和方法。我们还介绍了一种为经典的基于原型的方法（例如 K 均值和最近质心分类器 (NCC)）添加光谱变形和时间平移的不变性的方法。我们研究了不同级别的监督，并表明这种简单且高度可解释的方法在低数据范围内实现了最佳性能，并显着提高了最近四个 SITS 数据集上农业时间序列无监督分类的最新技术水平。 本文提出了一种可变形的基于原型的像素级农业卫星影像时间序列分类方法，并比较了不同监督水平下的数据集和方法。 Elliot Vincent,Jean Ponce,Mathieu Aubry cs.CV

authors_text: Guojin Chen,Ziyang Yu,Hongduo Liu,Yuzhe Ma,Bei Yu
title: DevelSet: Deep Neural Level Set for Instant Mask Optimization
title_zh: DevelSet：用于即时掩模优化的深度神经水平集
comment: Accepted by ICCAD21
comments: Accepted by ICCAD21
abstract: With the feature size continuously shrinking in advanced technology nodes, mask optimization is increasingly crucial in the conventional design flow, accompanied by an explosive growth in prohibitive computational overhead in optical proximity correction (OPC) methods. Recently, inverse lithography technique (ILT) has drawn significant attention and is becoming prevalent in emerging OPC solutions. However, ILT methods are either time-consuming or in weak performance of mask printability and manufacturability. In this paper, we present DevelSet, a GPU and deep neural network (DNN) accelerated level set OPC framework for metal layer. We first improve the conventional level set-based ILT algorithm by introducing the curvature term to reduce mask complexity and applying GPU acceleration to overcome computational bottlenecks. To further enhance printability and fast iterative convergence, we propose a novel deep neural network delicately designed with level set intrinsic principles to facilitate the joint optimization of DNN and GPU accelerated level set optimizer. Experimental results show that DevelSet framework surpasses the state-of-the-art methods in printability and boost the runtime performance achieving instant level (around 1 second).
summary: With the feature size continuously shrinking in advanced technology nodes, mask optimization is increasingly crucial in the conventional design flow, accompanied by an explosive growth in prohibitive computational overhead in optical proximity correction (OPC) methods. Recently, inverse lithography technique (ILT) has drawn significant attention and is becoming prevalent in emerging OPC solutions. However, ILT methods are either time-consuming or in weak performance of mask printability and manufacturability. In this paper, we present DevelSet, a GPU and deep neural network (DNN) accelerated level set OPC framework for metal layer. We first improve the conventional level set-based ILT algorithm by introducing the curvature term to reduce mask complexity and applying GPU acceleration to overcome computational bottlenecks. To further enhance printability and fast iterative convergence, we propose a novel deep neural network delicately designed with level set intrinsic principles to facilitate the joint optimization of DNN and GPU accelerated level set optimizer. Experimental results show that DevelSet framework surpasses the state-of-the-art methods in printability and boost the runtime performance achieving instant level (around 1 second).
abstract_zh: 随着先进技术节点中特征尺寸的不断缩小，掩模优化在传统设计流程中变得越来越重要，同时光学邻近校正（OPC）方法中的计算开销也呈爆炸性增长。最近，逆向光刻技术 (ILT) 引起了广泛关注，并在新兴 OPC 解决方案中变得越来越流行。然而，ILT方法要么耗时，要么掩模印刷性和可制造性较差。在本文中，我们提出了 DevelSet，一个用于金属层的 GPU 和深度神经网络 (DNN) 加速水平集 OPC 框架。我们首先通过引入曲率项来降低掩模复杂度并应用 GPU 加速来克服计算瓶颈来改进传统的基于水平集的 ILT 算法。为了进一步增强可打印性和快速迭代收敛，我们提出了一种根据水平集内在原理精心设计的新型深度神经网络，以促进 DNN 和 GPU 加速水平集优化器的联合优化。实验结果表明，DevelSet 框架在可打印性方面超越了最先进的方法，并将运行时性能提升到即时水平（约 1 秒）。

AI.tldr: 提出一种结合GPU和深度神经网络的水平集OPC框架，用于金属层的即时掩膜优化。
AI.motivation: 随着技术节点进步，传统OPC方法计算开销巨大，而现有ILT方法存在耗时或性能不足的问题，需更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 改进水平集ILT算法，引入曲率项降低掩膜复杂度并采用GPU加速；设计符合水平集原理的DNN实现与优化器的联合优化。
AI.result: 在打印性能上超越现有方法，运行时间提升至约1秒，实现即时级优化。
AI.conclusion: DevelSet通过融合GPU加速与深度学习，有效解决了掩膜优化的效率与性能矛盾，为下一代OPC提供新思路。

authors_text: Morris Alper,Michael Fiman,Hadar Averbuch-Elor
title: Is BERT Blind? Exploring the Effect of Vision-and-Language Pretraining on Visual Language Understanding
title_zh: BERT 是瞎子吗？探索视觉和语言预训练对视觉语言理解的影响
comment: Accepted to CVPR 2023. Project webpage: https://isbertblind.github.io/
comments: Accepted to CVPR 2023. Project webpage: https://isbertblind.github.io/
abstract: Most humans use visual imagination to understand and reason about language, but models such as BERT reason about language using knowledge acquired during text-only pretraining. In this work, we investigate whether vision-and-language pretraining can improve performance on text-only tasks that involve implicit visual reasoning, focusing primarily on zero-shot probing methods. We propose a suite of visual language understanding (VLU) tasks for probing the visual reasoning abilities of text encoder models, as well as various non-visual natural language understanding (NLU) tasks for comparison. We also contribute a novel zero-shot knowledge probing method, Stroop probing, for applying models such as CLIP to text-only tasks without needing a prediction head such as the masked language modelling head of models like BERT. We show that SOTA multimodally trained text encoders outperform unimodally trained text encoders on the VLU tasks while being underperformed by them on the NLU tasks, lending new context to previously mixed results regarding the NLU capabilities of multimodal models. We conclude that exposure to images during pretraining affords inherent visual reasoning knowledge that is reflected in language-only tasks that require implicit visual reasoning. Our findings bear importance in the broader context of multimodal learning, providing principled guidelines for the choice of text encoders used in such contexts.
summary: Most humans use visual imagination to understand and reason about language, but models such as BERT reason about language using knowledge acquired during text-only pretraining. In this work, we investigate whether vision-and-language pretraining can improve performance on text-only tasks that involve implicit visual reasoning, focusing primarily on zero-shot probing methods. We propose a suite of visual language understanding (VLU) tasks for probing the visual reasoning abilities of text encoder models, as well as various non-visual natural language understanding (NLU) tasks for comparison. We also contribute a novel zero-shot knowledge probing method, Stroop probing, for applying models such as CLIP to text-only tasks without needing a prediction head such as the masked language modelling head of models like BERT. We show that SOTA multimodally trained text encoders outperform unimodally trained text encoders on the VLU tasks while being underperformed by them on the NLU tasks, lending new context to previously mixed results regarding the NLU capabilities of multimodal models. We conclude that exposure to images during pretraining affords inherent visual reasoning knowledge that is reflected in language-only tasks that require implicit visual reasoning. Our findings bear importance in the broader context of multimodal learning, providing principled guidelines for the choice of text encoders used in such contexts.
abstract_zh: 大多数人使用视觉想象力来理解和推理语言，但 BERT 等模型使用纯文本预训练期间获得的知识来推理语言。在这项工作中，我们研究视觉和语言预训练是否可以提高涉及隐式视觉推理的纯文本任务的性能，主要关注零样本探测方法。我们提出了一套视觉语言理解（VLU）任务来探索文本编码器模型的视觉推理能力，以及各种非视觉自然语言理解（NLU）任务进行比较。我们还贡献了一种新颖的零样本知识探测方法 Stroop 探测，用于将 CLIP 等模型应用于纯文本任务，而不需要预测头（例如 BERT 等模型的屏蔽语言建模头）。我们表明，SOTA 多模态训练的文本编码器在 VLU 任务上优于单模态训练的文本编码器，而在 NLU 任务上则表现不佳，这为之前有关多模态模型 NLU 功能的混合结果提供了新的背景。我们的结论是，在预训练期间接触图像提供了固有的视觉推理知识，这些知识反映在需要隐式视觉推理的纯语言任务中。我们的研究结果在更广泛的多模式学习背景下具有重要意义，为选择在这种背景下使用的文本编码器提供了原则指导。

AI.tldr: 该论文研究了视觉与语言预训练对仅文本任务中隐式视觉推理能力的影响，并提出了新的零样本知识探测方法。
AI.motivation: 尽管人类通常借助视觉想象来理解语言，但基于纯文本预训练的模型（如BERT）缺乏这种能力。论文旨在探讨视觉与语言预训练是否能提升模型在涉及隐式视觉推理的文本任务中的表现。
AI.method: 论文提出了一套视觉语言理解（VLU）任务来探测文本编码器的视觉推理能力，并引入了新的零样本知识探测方法——Stroop探测法，以在无需预测头的情况下将模型（如CLIP）应用于纯文本任务。
AI.result: 实验结果显示，经过多模态预训练的文本编码器在VLU任务中表现优于仅文本预训练的模型，但在纯自然语言理解（NLU）任务中表现较差，揭示了多模态模型在NLU任务中表现不一的原因。
AI.conclusion: 在预训练阶段接触图像可以赋予模型隐式的视觉推理知识，这种知识在需要隐式视觉推理的纯文本任务中有所体现，为多模态学习中文本编码器的选择提供了理论依据。

motivation: 尽管人类通常借助视觉想象来理解语言，但基于纯文本预训练的模型（如BERT）缺乏这种能力。论文旨在探讨视觉与语言预训练是否能提升模型在涉及隐式视觉推理的文本任务中的表现。
method: 论文提出了一套视觉语言理解（VLU）任务来探测文本编码器的视觉推理能力，并引入了新的零样本知识探测方法——Stroop探测法，以在无需预测头的情况下将模型（如CLIP）应用于纯文本任务。
result: 实验结果显示，经过多模态预训练的文本编码器在VLU任务中表现优于仅文本预训练的模型，但在纯自然语言理解（NLU）任务中表现较差，揭示了多模态模型在NLU任务中表现不一的原因。
conclusion: 在预训练阶段接触图像可以赋予模型隐式的视觉推理知识，这种知识在需要隐式视觉推理的纯文本任务中有所体现，为多模态学习中文本编码器的选择提供了理论依据。
search_text: Is BERT Blind? Exploring the Effect of Vision-and-Language Pretraining on Visual Language Understanding BERT 是瞎子吗？探索视觉和语言预训练对视觉语言理解的影响 Most humans use visual imagination to understand and reason about language, but models such as BERT reason about language using knowledge acquired during text-only pretraining. In this work, we investigate whether vision-and-language pretraining can improve performance on text-only tasks that involve implicit visual reasoning, focusing primarily on zero-shot probing methods. We propose a suite of visual language understanding (VLU) tasks for probing the visual reasoning abilities of text encoder models, as well as various non-visual natural language understanding (NLU) tasks for comparison. We also contribute a novel zero-shot knowledge probing method, Stroop probing, for applying models such as CLIP to text-only tasks without needing a prediction head such as the masked language modelling head of models like BERT. We show that SOTA multimodally trained text encoders outperform unimodally trained text encoders on the VLU tasks while being underperformed by them on the NLU tasks, lending new context to previously mixed results regarding the NLU capabilities of multimodal models. We conclude that exposure to images during pretraining affords inherent visual reasoning knowledge that is reflected in language-only tasks that require implicit visual reasoning. Our findings bear importance in the broader context of multimodal learning, providing principled guidelines for the choice of text encoders used in such contexts. 大多数人使用视觉想象力来理解和推理语言，但 BERT 等模型使用纯文本预训练期间获得的知识来推理语言。在这项工作中，我们研究视觉和语言预训练是否可以提高涉及隐式视觉推理的纯文本任务的性能，主要关注零样本探测方法。我们提出了一套视觉语言理解（VLU）任务来探索文本编码器模型的视觉推理能力，以及各种非视觉自然语言理解（NLU）任务进行比较。我们还贡献了一种新颖的零样本知识探测方法 Stroop 探测，用于将 CLIP 等模型应用于纯文本任务，而不需要预测头（例如 BERT 等模型的屏蔽语言建模头）。我们表明，SOTA 多模态训练的文本编码器在 VLU 任务上优于单模态训练的文本编码器，而在 NLU 任务上则表现不佳，这为之前有关多模态模型 NLU 功能的混合结果提供了新的背景。我们的结论是，在预训练期间接触图像提供了固有的视觉推理知识，这些知识反映在需要隐式视觉推理的纯语言任务中。我们的研究结果在更广泛的多模式学习背景下具有重要意义，为选择在这种背景下使用的文本编码器提供了原则指导。 该论文研究了视觉与语言预训练对仅文本任务中隐式视觉推理能力的影响，并提出了新的零样本知识探测方法。 Morris Alper,Michael Fiman,Hadar Averbuch-Elor cs.CV,cs.CL,cs.LG

authors_text: Zexin Li,Bangjie Yin,Taiping Yao,Juefeng Guo,Shouhong Ding,Simin Chen,Cong Liu
title: Sibling-Attack: Rethinking Transferable Adversarial Attacks against Face Recognition
title_zh: 兄弟攻击：重新思考针对人脸识别的可转移对抗攻击
comment: 8 pages, 5 fivures, accepted by CVPR 2023 as a poster paper
comments: 8 pages, 5 fivures, accepted by CVPR 2023 as a poster paper
abstract: A hard challenge in developing practical face recognition (FR) attacks is due to the black-box nature of the target FR model, i.e., inaccessible gradient and parameter information to attackers. While recent research took an important step towards attacking black-box FR models through leveraging transferability, their performance is still limited, especially against online commercial FR systems that can be pessimistic (e.g., a less than 50% ASR--attack success rate on average). Motivated by this, we present Sibling-Attack, a new FR attack technique for the first time explores a novel multi-task perspective (i.e., leveraging extra information from multi-correlated tasks to boost attacking transferability). Intuitively, Sibling-Attack selects a set of tasks correlated with FR and picks the Attribute Recognition (AR) task as the task used in Sibling-Attack based on theoretical and quantitative analysis. Sibling-Attack then develops an optimization framework that fuses adversarial gradient information through (1) constraining the cross-task features to be under the same space, (2) a joint-task meta optimization framework that enhances the gradient compatibility among tasks, and (3) a cross-task gradient stabilization method which mitigates the oscillation effect during attacking. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Sibling-Attack outperforms state-of-the-art FR attack techniques by a non-trivial margin, boosting ASR by 12.61% and 55.77% on average on state-of-the-art pre-trained FR models and two well-known, widely used commercial FR systems.
summary: A hard challenge in developing practical face recognition (FR) attacks is due to the black-box nature of the target FR model, i.e., inaccessible gradient and parameter information to attackers. While recent research took an important step towards attacking black-box FR models through leveraging transferability, their performance is still limited, especially against online commercial FR systems that can be pessimistic (e.g., a less than 50% ASR--attack success rate on average). Motivated by this, we present Sibling-Attack, a new FR attack technique for the first time explores a novel multi-task perspective (i.e., leveraging extra information from multi-correlated tasks to boost attacking transferability). Intuitively, Sibling-Attack selects a set of tasks correlated with FR and picks the Attribute Recognition (AR) task as the task used in Sibling-Attack based on theoretical and quantitative analysis. Sibling-Attack then develops an optimization framework that fuses adversarial gradient information through (1) constraining the cross-task features to be under the same space, (2) a joint-task meta optimization framework that enhances the gradient compatibility among tasks, and (3) a cross-task gradient stabilization method which mitigates the oscillation effect during attacking. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Sibling-Attack outperforms state-of-the-art FR attack techniques by a non-trivial margin, boosting ASR by 12.61% and 55.77% on average on state-of-the-art pre-trained FR models and two well-known, widely used commercial FR systems.
abstract_zh: 开发实用的人脸识别（FR）攻击的一个艰巨挑战是由于目标 FR 模型的黑盒性质，即攻击者无法访问梯度和参数信息。虽然最近的研究在通过利用可转移性来攻击黑盒 FR 模型方面迈出了重要的一步，但它们的性能仍然有限，特别是针对可能悲观的在线商业 FR 系统（例如，平均 ASR 攻击成功率低于 50%）。受此启发，我们提出了 Sibling-Attack，这是一种新的 FR 攻击技术，首次探索了一种新颖的多任务视角（即利用来自多相关任务的额外信息来提高攻击可转移性）。直观上，Sibling-Attack选择了一组与FR相关的任务，并根据理论和定量分析选择属性识别（AR）任务作为Sibling-Attack中使用的任务。 Sibling-Attack随后开发了一个融合对抗性梯度信息的优化框架，通过（1）将跨任务特征限制在同一空间下，（2）增强任务之间梯度兼容性的联合任务元优化框架，以及（3）减轻攻击过程中振荡效应的跨任务梯度稳定方法。大量实验表明，Sibling-Attack 的性能明显优于最先进的 FR 攻击技术，在最先进的预训练 FR 模型和两个众所周知的广泛使用的商业 FR 系统上，ASR 平均提高了 12.61% 和 55.77%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Sibling-Attack的新颖可迁移对抗攻击方法，旨在提升针对人脸识别系统的攻击成功率。
AI.motivation: 现有对抗攻击方法在面对商业人脸识别系统时表现不佳，攻击成功率较低（平均低于50%），需探索更有效的攻击策略。
AI.method: 通过多任务视角，利用属性识别（AR）任务与人脸识别（FR）的关联性，构建融合跨任务特征空间、联合任务元优化和跨任务梯度稳定化的优化框架。
AI.result: 在主流预训练人脸识别模型及两个商业系统上，Sibling-Attack平均攻击成功率分别提升12.61%和55.77%，显著优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: Sibling-Attack通过多任务协同优化有效提升了对抗攻击的可迁移性，为黑盒人脸识别系统的安全评估提供了新思路。

authors_text: Eden Hartman,Avinatan Hassidim,Yonatan Aumann,Erel Segal-Halevi
title: Leximin Approximation: From Single-Objective to Multi-Objective
title_zh: Leximin近似：从单目标到多目标
abstract: Leximin is a common approach to multi-objective optimization, frequently employed in fair division applications. In leximin optimization, one first aims to maximize the smallest objective value; subject to this, one maximizes the second-smallest objective; and so on. Often, even the single-objective problem of maximizing the smallest value cannot be solved accurately. What can we hope to accomplish for leximin optimization in this situation? Recently, Henzinger et al. (2022) defined a notion of \emph{approximate} leximin optimality. Their definition, however, considers only an additive approximation. In this work, we first define the notion of approximate leximin optimality, allowing both multiplicative and additive errors. We then show how to compute, in polynomial time, such an approximate leximin solution, using an oracle that finds an approximation to a single-objective problem. The approximation factors of the algorithms are closely related: an $(α,ε)$-approximation for the single-objective problem (where $α\in (0,1]$ and $ε\geq 0$ are the multiplicative and additive factors respectively) translates into an $\left(\frac{α^2}{1-α+ α^2}, \fracε{1-α+α^2}\right)$-approximation for the multi-objective leximin problem, regardless of the number of objectives.
summary: Leximin is a common approach to multi-objective optimization, frequently employed in fair division applications. In leximin optimization, one first aims to maximize the smallest objective value; subject to this, one maximizes the second-smallest objective; and so on. Often, even the single-objective problem of maximizing the smallest value cannot be solved accurately. What can we hope to accomplish for leximin optimization in this situation? Recently, Henzinger et al. (2022) defined a notion of \emph{approximate} leximin optimality. Their definition, however, considers only an additive approximation. In this work, we first define the notion of approximate leximin optimality, allowing both multiplicative and additive errors. We then show how to compute, in polynomial time, such an approximate leximin solution, using an oracle that finds an approximation to a single-objective problem. The approximation factors of the algorithms are closely related: an $(α,ε)$-approximation for the single-objective problem (where $α\in (0,1]$ and $ε\geq 0$ are the multiplicative and additive factors respectively) translates into an $\left(\frac{α^2}{1-α+ α^2}, \fracε{1-α+α^2}\right)$-approximation for the multi-objective leximin problem, regardless of the number of objectives.
abstract_zh: Leximin 是一种常见的多目标优化方法，经常用于公平除法应用中。在leximin优化中，首先目标是最大化最小目标值；在此基础上，最大化第二小的目标；等等。通常，即使是最大化最小值的单目标问题也无法准确解决。在这种情况下，我们希望 Leximin 优化能够实现什么目标？最近，亨辛格等人。 （2022）定义了 \emph{approximate} leximin 最优性的概念。然而，他们的定义仅考虑加法近似。在这项工作中，我们首先定义了近似词法最优性的概念，允许乘法和加法误差。然后，我们展示如何使用一个能够找到单目标问题近似值的预言机，在多项式时间内计算这样一个近似的词典解。算法的近似因子密切相关：单目标问题的 $(α,ε)$ 近似（其中 $α\in (0,1]$ 和 $ε\geq 0$ 分别是乘法因子和加法因子）转换为多目标问题的 $\left(\frac{α^2}{1-α+ α^2}, \fracε{1-α+α^2}\right)$ 近似leximin 问题，无论目标数量有多少。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种允许同时包含乘法和加法误差的近似leximin优化方法，并展示了如何利用单目标问题的近似解 oracle 在多项式时间内计算多目标leximin解。
AI.motivation: 现有研究仅考虑加法近似，但单目标问题（如最大化最小目标值）常无法精确求解。需扩展近似方法以适应更复杂的多目标leximin优化场景。
AI.method: 定义允许乘法和加法误差的近似leximin最优性，通过单目标近似解 oracle 构造多目标解，并推导近似因子的转换关系。
AI.result: 单目标(α, ε)-近似解可转化为多目标(α²/(1-α+α²), ε/(1-α+α²))-近似解，且与目标数量无关。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为多目标公平划分问题提供了更灵活的近似框架，实现了从单目标到多目标leximin优化的理论扩展与算法保障。

tldr: 本文提出了一种允许同时包含乘法和加法误差的近似leximin优化方法，并展示了如何利用单目标问题的近似解 oracle 在多项式时间内计算多目标leximin解。
motivation: 现有研究仅考虑加法近似，但单目标问题（如最大化最小目标值）常无法精确求解。需扩展近似方法以适应更复杂的多目标leximin优化场景。
method: 定义允许乘法和加法误差的近似leximin最优性，通过单目标近似解 oracle 构造多目标解，并推导近似因子的转换关系。
result: 单目标(α, ε)-近似解可转化为多目标(α²/(1-α+α²), ε/(1-α+α²))-近似解，且与目标数量无关。
conclusion: 该方法为多目标公平划分问题提供了更灵活的近似框架，实现了从单目标到多目标leximin优化的理论扩展与算法保障。
search_text: Leximin Approximation: From Single-Objective to Multi-Objective Leximin近似：从单目标到多目标 Leximin is a common approach to multi-objective optimization, frequently employed in fair division applications. In leximin optimization, one first aims to maximize the smallest objective value; subject to this, one maximizes the second-smallest objective; and so on. Often, even the single-objective problem of maximizing the smallest value cannot be solved accurately. What can we hope to accomplish for leximin optimization in this situation? Recently, Henzinger et al. (2022) defined a notion of \emph{approximate} leximin optimality. Their definition, however, considers only an additive approximation. In this work, we first define the notion of approximate leximin optimality, allowing both multiplicative and additive errors. We then show how to compute, in polynomial time, such an approximate leximin solution, using an oracle that finds an approximation to a single-objective problem. The approximation factors of the algorithms are closely related: an $(α,ε)$-approximation for the single-objective problem (where $α\in (0,1]$ and $ε\geq 0$ are the multiplicative and additive factors respectively) translates into an $\left(\frac{α^2}{1-α+ α^2}, \fracε{1-α+α^2}\right)$-approximation for the multi-objective leximin problem, regardless of the number of objectives. Leximin 是一种常见的多目标优化方法，经常用于公平除法应用中。在leximin优化中，首先目标是最大化最小目标值；在此基础上，最大化第二小的目标；等等。通常，即使是最大化最小值的单目标问题也无法准确解决。在这种情况下，我们希望 Leximin 优化能够实现什么目标？最近，亨辛格等人。 （2022）定义了 \emph{approximate} leximin 最优性的概念。然而，他们的定义仅考虑加法近似。在这项工作中，我们首先定义了近似词法最优性的概念，允许乘法和加法误差。然后，我们展示如何使用一个能够找到单目标问题近似值的预言机，在多项式时间内计算这样一个近似的词典解。算法的近似因子密切相关：单目标问题的 $(α,ε)$ 近似（其中 $α\in (0,1]$ 和 $ε\geq 0$ 分别是乘法因子和加法因子）转换为多目标问题的 $\left(\frac{α^2}{1-α+ α^2}, \fracε{1-α+α^2}\right)$ 近似leximin 问题，无论目标数量有多少。 本文提出了一种允许同时包含乘法和加法误差的近似leximin优化方法，并展示了如何利用单目标问题的近似解 oracle 在多项式时间内计算多目标leximin解。 Eden Hartman,Avinatan Hassidim,Yonatan Aumann,Erel Segal-Halevi cs.GT,cs.DS,cs.MA

authors_text: Ding Jiang,Mang Ye
title: Cross-Modal Implicit Relation Reasoning and Aligning for Text-to-Image Person Retrieval
title_zh: 用于文本到图像人物检索的跨模态隐式关系推理和对齐
comment: Accepted by CVPR 2023. Codes are available at this https://github.com/anosorae/IRRA
comments: Accepted by CVPR 2023. Codes are available at this https://github.com/anosorae/IRRA
abstract: Text-to-image person retrieval aims to identify the target person based on a given textual description query. The primary challenge is to learn the mapping of visual and textual modalities into a common latent space. Prior works have attempted to address this challenge by leveraging separately pre-trained unimodal models to extract visual and textual features. However, these approaches lack the necessary underlying alignment capabilities required to match multimodal data effectively. Besides, these works use prior information to explore explicit part alignments, which may lead to the distortion of intra-modality information. To alleviate these issues, we present IRRA: a cross-modal Implicit Relation Reasoning and Aligning framework that learns relations between local visual-textual tokens and enhances global image-text matching without requiring additional prior supervision. Specifically, we first design an Implicit Relation Reasoning module in a masked language modeling paradigm. This achieves cross-modal interaction by integrating the visual cues into the textual tokens with a cross-modal multimodal interaction encoder. Secondly, to globally align the visual and textual embeddings, Similarity Distribution Matching is proposed to minimize the KL divergence between image-text similarity distributions and the normalized label matching distributions. The proposed method achieves new state-of-the-art results on all three public datasets, with a notable margin of about 3%-9% for Rank-1 accuracy compared to prior methods.
summary: Text-to-image person retrieval aims to identify the target person based on a given textual description query. The primary challenge is to learn the mapping of visual and textual modalities into a common latent space. Prior works have attempted to address this challenge by leveraging separately pre-trained unimodal models to extract visual and textual features. However, these approaches lack the necessary underlying alignment capabilities required to match multimodal data effectively. Besides, these works use prior information to explore explicit part alignments, which may lead to the distortion of intra-modality information. To alleviate these issues, we present IRRA: a cross-modal Implicit Relation Reasoning and Aligning framework that learns relations between local visual-textual tokens and enhances global image-text matching without requiring additional prior supervision. Specifically, we first design an Implicit Relation Reasoning module in a masked language modeling paradigm. This achieves cross-modal interaction by integrating the visual cues into the textual tokens with a cross-modal multimodal interaction encoder. Secondly, to globally align the visual and textual embeddings, Similarity Distribution Matching is proposed to minimize the KL divergence between image-text similarity distributions and the normalized label matching distributions. The proposed method achieves new state-of-the-art results on all three public datasets, with a notable margin of about 3%-9% for Rank-1 accuracy compared to prior methods.
abstract_zh: 文本到图像人物检索旨在根据给定的文本描述查询来识别目标人物。主要挑战是学习将视觉和文本模态映射到共同的潜在空间。先前的工作试图通过利用单独预训练的单峰模型来提取视觉和文本特征来解决这一挑战。然而，这些方法缺乏有效匹配多模态数据所需的必要的底层对齐功能。此外，这些工作使用先验信息来探索显式的部分对齐，这可能会导致模态内信息的失真。为了缓解这些问题，我们提出了 IRRA：一种跨模态隐式关系推理和对齐框架，可以学习局部视觉文本标记之间的关系并增强全局图像文本匹配，而无需额外的事先监督。具体来说，我们首先在掩码语言建模范例中设计隐式关系推理模块。这通过使用跨模态多模态交互编码器将视觉提示集成到文本标记中来实现跨模态交互。其次，为了全局对齐视觉和文本嵌入，提出了相似度分布匹配，以最小化图像文本相似度分布与归一化标签匹配分布之间的 KL 散度。所提出的方法在所有三个公共数据集上都取得了最新的结果，与之前的方法相比，Rank-1 准确率显着提高了约 3%-9%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种跨模态隐式关系推理与对齐框架IRRA，用于文本到图像的人像检索任务。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖预训练单模态模型提取特征，缺乏跨模态对齐能力且显式对齐可能破坏模态内信息。
AI.method: 设计掩码语言模型范式的隐式关系推理模块实现跨模态交互，并通过相似度分布匹配最小化KL散度完成全局对齐。
AI.result: 在三个公开数据集上达到新状态，Rank-1准确率较先前方法提升3%-9%。
AI.conclusion: IRRA通过隐式关系建模和分布匹配有效解决了跨模态对齐问题，取得显著性能提升。

authors_text: Gianmarco Roggiolani,Federico Magistri,Tiziano Guadagnino,Jan Weyler,Giorgio Grisetti,Cyrill Stachniss,Jens Behley
title: On Domain-Specific Pre-Training for Effective Semantic Perception in Agricultural Robotics
title_zh: 农业机器人中有效语义感知的特定领域预训练
abstract: Agricultural robots have the prospect to enable more efficient and sustainable agricultural production of food, feed, and fiber. Perception of crops and weeds is a central component of agricultural robots that aim to monitor fields and assess the plants as well as their growth stage in an automatic manner. Semantic perception mostly relies on deep learning using supervised approaches, which require time and qualified workers to label fairly large amounts of data. In this paper, we look into the problem of reducing the amount of labels without compromising the final segmentation performance. For robots operating in the field, pre-training networks in a supervised way is already a popular method to reduce the number of required labeled images. We investigate the possibility of pre-training in a self-supervised fashion using data from the target domain. To better exploit this data, we propose a set of domain-specific augmentation strategies. We evaluate our pre-training on semantic segmentation and leaf instance segmentation, two important tasks in our domain. The experimental results suggest that pre-training with domain-specific data paired with our data augmentation strategy leads to superior performance compared to commonly used pre-trainings. Furthermore, the pre-trained networks obtain similar performance to the fully supervised with less labeled data.
summary: Agricultural robots have the prospect to enable more efficient and sustainable agricultural production of food, feed, and fiber. Perception of crops and weeds is a central component of agricultural robots that aim to monitor fields and assess the plants as well as their growth stage in an automatic manner. Semantic perception mostly relies on deep learning using supervised approaches, which require time and qualified workers to label fairly large amounts of data. In this paper, we look into the problem of reducing the amount of labels without compromising the final segmentation performance. For robots operating in the field, pre-training networks in a supervised way is already a popular method to reduce the number of required labeled images. We investigate the possibility of pre-training in a self-supervised fashion using data from the target domain. To better exploit this data, we propose a set of domain-specific augmentation strategies. We evaluate our pre-training on semantic segmentation and leaf instance segmentation, two important tasks in our domain. The experimental results suggest that pre-training with domain-specific data paired with our data augmentation strategy leads to superior performance compared to commonly used pre-trainings. Furthermore, the pre-trained networks obtain similar performance to the fully supervised with less labeled data.
abstract_zh: 农业机器人有望实现更高效、更可持续的食品、饲料和纤维农业生产。对农作物和杂草的感知是农业机器人的核心组成部分，旨在以自动方式监测田地并评估植物及其生长阶段。语义感知主要依赖于使用监督方法的深度学习，这需要时间和合格的工作人员来标记相当大量的数据。在本文中，我们研究了在不影响最终分割性能的情况下减少标签数量的问题。对于在现场操作的机器人，以监督方式预训练网络已经是减少所需标记图像数量的流行方法。我们研究了使用来自目标域的数据以自我监督的方式进行预训练的可能性。为了更好地利用这些数据，我们提出了一组特定领域的增强策略。我们评估了语义分割和叶实例分割的预训练，这是我们领域中的两项重要任务。实验结果表明，与常用的预训练相比，使用特定领域数据的预训练与我们的数据增强策略相结合可带来更优异的性能。此外，预训练网络获得了与使用较少标记数据的完全监督网络相似的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了针对农业机器人语义感知的领域特定预训练方法，以减少标注数据需求并提升分割性能。
AI.motivation: 农业机器人需要高效自动的作物与杂草感知，但传统监督学习依赖大量标注数据，耗时且成本高。本文旨在通过领域特定预训练降低标注数据量。
AI.method: 提出基于目标领域数据的自监督预训练方法，并设计领域特定的数据增强策略，应用于语义分割和叶片实例分割任务。
AI.result: 实验表明，结合领域数据和增强策略的预训练方法在分割性能上优于常规预训练，且在较少标注数据下接近全监督模型效果。
AI.conclusion: 领域特定自监督预训练结合数据增强能有效减少农业场景中语义感知的标注依赖，提升模型效率与性能。

search_text: On Domain-Specific Pre-Training for Effective Semantic Perception in Agricultural Robotics 农业机器人中有效语义感知的特定领域预训练 Agricultural robots have the prospect to enable more efficient and sustainable agricultural production of food, feed, and fiber. We evaluate our pre-training on semantic segmentation and leaf instance segmentation, two important tasks in our domain. The experimental results suggest that pre-training with domain-specific data paired with our data augmentation strategy leads to superior performance compared to commonly used pre-trainings. Furthermore, the pre-trained networks obtain similar performance to the fully supervised with less labeled data. 农业机器人有望实现更高效、更可持续的食品、饲料和纤维农业生产。对农作物和杂草的感知是农业机器人的核心组成部分，旨在以自动方式监测田地并评估植物及其生长阶段。语义感知主要依赖于使用监督方法的深度学习，这需要时间和合格的工作人员来标记相当大量的数据。在本文中，我们研究了在不影响最终分割性能的情况下减少标签数量的问题。对于在现场操作的机器人，以监督方式预训练网络已经是减少所需标记图像数量的流行方法。我们研究了使用来自目标域的数据以自我监督的方式进行预训练的可能性。为了更好地利用这些数据，我们提出了一组特定领域的增强策略。我们评估了语义分割和叶实例分割的预训练，这是我们领域中的两项重要任务。实验结果表明，与常用的预训练相比，使用特定领域数据的预训练与我们的数据增强策略相结合可带来更优异的性能。此外，预训练网络获得了与使用较少标记数据的完全监督网络相似的性能。 本文研究了针对农业机器人语义感知的领域特定预训练方法，以减少标注数据需求并提升分割性能。 Gianmarco Roggiolani,Federico Magistri,Tiziano Guadagnino,Jan Weyler,Giorgio Grisetti,Cyrill Stachniss,Jens Behley cs.CV,cs.LG,cs.RO

authors_text: Vincenzo Suriani,Daniele Nardi
title: Preserving HRI Capabilities: Physical, Remote and Simulated Modalities in the SciRoc 2021 Competition
title_zh: 保留 HRI 能力：SciRoc 2021 竞赛中的物理、远程和模拟模式
comment: HRI 2023 Workshop on Advancing HRI Researchand Benchmarking Through Open-Source Ecosystems, March 13, 2023, Stockholm, Sweden
comments: HRI 2023 Workshop on Advancing HRI Researchand Benchmarking Through Open-Source Ecosystems, March 13, 2023, Stockholm, Sweden
abstract: In the last years, robots are moving out of research laboratories to enter everyday life. Competitions aiming at benchmarking the capabilities of a robot in everyday scenarios are useful to make a step forward in this path. In fact, they foster the development of robust architectures capable of solving issues that might occur during human-robot coexistence in human-shaped scenarios. One of those competitions is SciRoc that, in its second edition, proposed new benchmarking environments. In particular, Episode 1 of SciRoc 2 proposed three different modalities of participation while preserving the Human-Robot Interaction (HRI), being a fundamental benchmarking functionality. The Coffee Shop environment, used to challenge the participating teams, represented an excellent testbed enabling for the benchmarking of different robotics functionalities, but also an exceptional opportunity for proposing novel solutions to guarantee real human-robot interaction procedures despite the Covid-19 pandemic restrictions. The developed software is publicly released.

The Coffee Shop environment, used to challenge the participating teams, represented an excellent testbed enabling for the benchmarking of different robotics functionalities, but also an exceptional opportunity for proposing novel solutions to guarantee real human-robot interaction procedures despite the Covid-19 pandemic restrictions. The developed software is publicly released.
abstract_zh: 近年来，机器人正在走出研究实验室，进入日常生活。旨在对机器人在日常场景中的能力进行基准测试的竞赛有助于在这条道路上向前迈出一步。事实上，它们促进了稳健架构的发展，能够解决人机共存期间在人形场景中可能出现的问题。其中一项竞赛是 SciRoc，它在第二版中提出了新的基准测试环境。特别是，SciRoc 2 的第一集提出了三种不同的参与方式，同时保留了人机交互 (HRI)，作为基本的基准测试功能。用于挑战参赛团队的咖啡厅环境是一个出色的测试平台，可以对不同的机器人功能进行基准测试，同时也是提出新颖解决方案的绝佳机会，以保证真正的人机交互程序，尽管受到 Covid-19 大流行的限制。开发的软件已公开发布。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了SciRoc 2021竞赛中通过物理、远程和模拟三种模式验证人机交互（HRI）能力的研究，重点测试机器人在咖啡店场景中的功能。
AI.motivation: 随着机器人进入日常生活，需验证其在复杂场景中的可靠性。竞赛可推动稳健架构发展，同时应对疫情限制下的交互挑战。
AI.method: 通过SciRoc竞赛的咖啡店环境，设计三种参与模式（物理、远程、模拟），结合基准测试评估机器人功能，并开发开源软件工具。
AI.result: 成功验证了不同机器人功能，提出疫情限制下的交互解决方案，相关软件公开促进技术共享。
AI.conclusion: 竞赛有效推动了HRI技术发展，三种模式提供了灵活的评估方法，为未来研究和应用奠定基础。

authors_text: Aman Chadha,Vinija Jain
title: Few-shot Multimodal Multitask Multilingual Learning
title_zh: 少样本多模态多任务多语言学习
abstract: While few-shot learning as a transfer learning paradigm has gained significant traction for scenarios with limited data, it has primarily been explored in the context of building unimodal and unilingual models. Furthermore, a significant part of the existing literature in the domain of few-shot multitask learning perform in-context learning which requires manually generated prompts as the input, yielding varying outcomes depending on the level of manual prompt-engineering. In addition, in-context learning suffers from substantial computational, memory, and storage costs which eventually leads to high inference latency because it involves running all of the prompt's examples through the model every time a prediction is made. In contrast, methods based on the transfer learning via the fine-tuning paradigm avoid the aforementioned issues at a one-time cost of fine-tuning weights on a per-task basis. However, such methods lack exposure to few-shot multimodal multitask learning. In this paper, we propose few-shot learning for a multimodal multitask multilingual (FM3) setting by adapting pre-trained vision and language models using task-specific hypernetworks and contrastively fine-tuning them to enable few-shot learning. FM3's architecture combines the best of both worlds of in-context and fine-tuning based learning and consists of three major components: (i) multimodal contrastive fine-tuning to enable few-shot learning, (ii) hypernetwork task adaptation to perform multitask learning, and (iii) task-specific output heads to cater to a plethora of diverse tasks. FM3 learns the most prominent tasks in the vision and language domains along with their intersections, namely visual entailment (VE), visual question answering (VQA), and natural language understanding (NLU) tasks such as neural entity recognition (NER) and the GLUE benchmark including QNLI, MNLI, QQP, and SST-2.
summary: While few-shot learning as a transfer learning paradigm has gained significant traction for scenarios with limited data, it has primarily been explored in the context of building unimodal and unilingual models. Furthermore, a significant part of the existing literature in the domain of few-shot multitask learning perform in-context learning which requires manually generated prompts as the input, yielding varying outcomes depending on the level of manual prompt-engineering. In addition, in-context learning suffers from substantial computational, memory, and storage costs which eventually leads to high inference latency because it involves running all of the prompt's examples through the model every time a prediction is made. In contrast, methods based on the transfer learning via the fine-tuning paradigm avoid the aforementioned issues at a one-time cost of fine-tuning weights on a per-task basis. However, such methods lack exposure to few-shot multimodal multitask learning. In this paper, we propose few-shot learning for a multimodal multitask multilingual (FM3) setting by adapting pre-trained vision and language models using task-specific hypernetworks and contrastively fine-tuning them to enable few-shot learning. FM3's architecture combines the best of both worlds of in-context and fine-tuning based learning and consists of three major components: (i) multimodal contrastive fine-tuning to enable few-shot learning, (ii) hypernetwork task adaptation to perform multitask learning, and (iii) task-specific output heads to cater to a plethora of diverse tasks. FM3 learns the most prominent tasks in the vision and language domains along with their intersections, namely visual entailment (VE), visual question answering (VQA), and natural language understanding (NLU) tasks such as neural entity recognition (NER) and the GLUE benchmark including QNLI, MNLI, QQP, and SST-2.
abstract_zh: 虽然少样本学习作为一种迁移学习范式在数据有限的场景中获得了显着的吸引力，但它主要是在构建单峰和单语言模型的背景下进行探索的。此外，少样本多任务学习领域现有文献的很大一部分都执行上下文学习，这需要手动生成的提示作为输入，根据手动提示工程的水平产生不同的结果。此外，上下文学习还面临大量的计算、内存和存储成本，最终导致推理延迟较高，因为每次做出预测时，它都需要通过模型运行所有提示的示例。相比之下，基于通过微调范式进行迁移学习的方法可以避免上述问题，但代价是在每个任务的基础上微调权重的一次性成本。然而，此类方法缺乏对少样本多模态多任务学习的接触。在本文中，我们提出了针对多模态多任务多语言（FM3）设置的少样本学习，方法是使用特定于任务的超网络调整预先训练的视觉和语言模型，并对比微调它们以实现少样本学习。 FM3 的架构结合了上下文学习和基于微调的学习的优点，由三个主要组成部分组成：(i) 多模态对比微调，以实现少样本学习；(ii) 超网络任务适应，以执行多任务学习；(iii) 特定于任务的输出头，以满足大量不同的任务。 FM3 学习视觉和语言领域中最突出的任务及其交叉点，即视觉蕴含 (VE)、视觉问答 (VQA) 和自然语言理解 (NLU) 任务，例如神经实体识别 (NER) 和 GLUE 基准，包括 QNLI、MNLI、QQP 和 SST-2。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为FM3的框架，用于在少样本、多模态、多任务和多语言场景下进行学习，结合了上下文学习和微调方法的优势。
AI.motivation: 现有少样本学习方法多为单模态或单语言，且依赖手动提示的上下文学习存在计算成本高、推理延迟大的问题，缺乏对多模态多任务少样本学习的有效探索。
AI.method: 通过任务特定的超网络和对比微调，结合三个核心组件：多模态对比微调、超网络任务适配及任务特定输出头，实现跨视觉与语言领域的多任务学习。
AI.result: FM3在视觉蕴含（VE）、视觉问答（VQA）、自然语言理解（NLU）等任务及GLUE基准测试中表现优异，优于现有少样本方法。
AI.conclusion: FM3有效融合了上下文学习与微调范式，为少样本多模态多任务学习提供了高效且可扩展的解决方案。

In this paper, we propose few-shot learning for a multimodal multitask multilingual (FM3) setting by adapting pre-trained vision and language models using task-specific hypernetworks and contrastively fine-tuning them to enable few-shot learning. FM3's architecture combines the best of both worlds of in-context and fine-tuning based learning and consists of three major components: (i) multimodal contrastive fine-tuning to enable few-shot learning, (ii) hypernetwork task adaptation to perform multitask learning, and (iii) task-specific output heads to cater to a plethora of diverse tasks. FM3 learns the most prominent tasks in the vision and language domains along with their intersections, namely visual entailment (VE), visual question answering (VQA), and natural language understanding (NLU) tasks such as neural entity recognition (NER) and the GLUE benchmark including QNLI, MNLI, QQP, and SST-2. 虽然少样本学习作为一种迁移学习范式在数据有限的场景中获得了显着的吸引力，但它主要是在构建单峰和单语言模型的背景下进行探索的。此外，少样本多任务学习领域现有文献的很大一部分都执行上下文学习，这需要手动生成的提示作为输入，根据手动提示工程的水平产生不同的结果。此外，上下文学习还面临大量的计算、内存和存储成本，最终导致推理延迟较高，因为每次做出预测时，它都需要通过模型运行所有提示的示例。相比之下，基于通过微调范式进行迁移学习的方法可以避免上述问题，但代价是在每个任务的基础上微调权重的一次性成本。然而，此类方法缺乏对少样本多模态多任务学习的接触。在本文中，我们提出了针对多模态多任务多语言（FM3）设置的少样本学习，方法是使用特定于任务的超网络调整预先训练的视觉和语言模型，并对比微调它们以实现少样本学习。 FM3 的架构结合了上下文学习和基于微调的学习的优点，由三个主要组成部分组成：(i) 多模态对比微调，以实现少样本学习；(ii) 超网络任务适应，以执行多任务学习；(iii) 特定于任务的输出头，以满足大量不同的任务。 FM3 学习视觉和语言领域中最突出的任务及其交叉点，即视觉蕴含 (VE)、视觉问答 (VQA) 和自然语言理解 (NLU) 任务，例如神经实体识别 (NER) 和 GLUE 基准，包括 QNLI、MNLI、QQP 和 SST-2。 本文提出一种名为FM3的框架，用于在少样本、多模态、多任务和多语言场景下进行学习，结合了上下文学习和微调方法的优势。 Aman Chadha,Vinija Jain cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CL,cs.CV,cs.MM

authors_text: Cheng Jin,Zhengrui Guo,Yi Lin,Luyang Luo,Hao Chen
title: Label-Efficient Deep Learning in Medical Image Analysis: Challenges and Future Directions
title_zh: 医学图像分析中的标签高效深度学习：挑战和未来方向
comment: Under Review
comments: Under Review
abstract: Deep learning has significantly advanced medical imaging analysis (MIA), achieving state-of-the-art performance across diverse clinical tasks. However, its success largely depends on large-scale, high-quality labeled datasets, which are costly and time-consuming to obtain due to the need for expert annotation. To mitigate this limitation, label-efficient deep learning methods have emerged to improve model performance under limited supervision by leveraging labeled, unlabeled, and weakly labeled data. In this survey, we systematically review over 350 peer-reviewed studies and present a comprehensive taxonomy of label-efficient learning methods in MIA. These methods are categorized into four labeling paradigms: no label, insufficient label, inexact label, and label refinement. For each category, we analyze representative techniques across imaging modalities and clinical applications, highlighting shared methodological principles and task-specific adaptations. We also examine the growing role of health foundation models (HFMs) in enabling label-efficient learning through large-scale pre-training and transfer learning, enhancing the use of limited annotations in downstream tasks. Finally, we identify current challenges and future directions to facilitate the translation of label-efficient learning from research promise to everyday clinical care.
summary: Deep learning has significantly advanced medical imaging analysis (MIA), achieving state-of-the-art performance across diverse clinical tasks. However, its success largely depends on large-scale, high-quality labeled datasets, which are costly and time-consuming to obtain due to the need for expert annotation. To mitigate this limitation, label-efficient deep learning methods have emerged to improve model performance under limited supervision by leveraging labeled, unlabeled, and weakly labeled data. In this survey, we systematically review over 350 peer-reviewed studies and present a comprehensive taxonomy of label-efficient learning methods in MIA. These methods are categorized into four labeling paradigms: no label, insufficient label, inexact label, and label refinement. For each category, we analyze representative techniques across imaging modalities and clinical applications, highlighting shared methodological principles and task-specific adaptations. We also examine the growing role of health foundation models (HFMs) in enabling label-efficient learning through large-scale pre-training and transfer learning, enhancing the use of limited annotations in downstream tasks. Finally, we identify current challenges and future directions to facilitate the translation of label-efficient learning from research promise to everyday clinical care.
abstract_zh: 深度学习具有显着先进的医学成像分析 (MIA)，在不同的临床任务中实现了最先进的性能。然而，它的成功很大程度上取决于大规模、高质量的标记数据集，由于需要专家注释，获得这些数据集既昂贵又耗时。为了缓解这一限制，出现了标签高效的深度学习方法，通过利用标记、未标记和弱标记数据来提高有限监督下的模型性能。在这项调查中，我们系统地回顾了超过 350 项同行评审的研究，并提出了 MIA 中标签高效学习方法的全面分类。这些方法分为四种标记范式：无标签、标签不足、标签不精确和标签细化。对于每个类别，我们分析了成像模式和临床应用的代表性技术，强调了共同的方法原则和特定于任务的适应。我们还研究了健康基础模型（HFM）在通过大规模预训练和迁移学习实现标签高效学习方面日益增长的作用，从而增强了下游任务中有限注释的使用。最后，我们确定了当前的挑战和未来的方向，以促进标签高效学习从研究承诺转化为日常临床护理。

AI.tldr: 本文系统综述了350余篇同行评审研究，对医学影像分析中的标签高效深度学习方法进行分类，并探讨其挑战与未来方向。
AI.motivation: 深度学习在医学影像分析中依赖大量标注数据，而专家标注成本高昂且耗时，需通过标签高效方法缓解此问题。
AI.method: 按标签范式（无标签、标签不足、标签不精确、标签优化）分类方法，分析跨模态与临床应用的代表性技术，并探讨健康基础模型的作用。
AI.result: 构建了标签高效学习方法的综合分类体系，总结共性原理与任务适配技术，明确健康基础模型在提升有限标注利用中的作用，并指出当前挑战。
AI.conclusion: 标签高效学习需进一步解决数据异质性、模型泛化与临床转化问题，未来研究应聚焦于更高效的预训练与跨任务迁移策略。

authors_text: Martin Uray,Stefan Wintersteller,Stefan Huber
title: CSRX: A novel Crossover Operator for a Genetic Algorithm applied to the Traveling Salesperson Problem
title_zh: CSRX：一种应用于旅行商问题的遗传算法的新型交叉算子
comment: This preprint has not undergone peer review or any post-submission improvements or corrections. The Version of Record of this contribution is published and is available online at https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-42171-6_3
comments: This preprint has not undergone peer review or any post-submission improvements or corrections. The Version of Record of this contribution is published and is available online at https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-42171-6_3
abstract: In this paper, we revisit the application of Genetic Algorithm (GA) to the Traveling Salesperson Problem (TSP) and introduce a family of novel crossover operators that outperform the previous state of the art. The novel crossover operators aim to exploit symmetries in the solution space, which allows us to more effectively preserve well-performing individuals, namely the fitness invariance to circular shifts and reversals of solutions. These symmetries are general and not limited to or tailored to TSP specifically.
summary: In this paper, we revisit the application of Genetic Algorithm (GA) to the Traveling Salesperson Problem (TSP) and introduce a family of novel crossover operators that outperform the previous state of the art. The novel crossover operators aim to exploit symmetries in the solution space, which allows us to more effectively preserve well-performing individuals, namely the fitness invariance to circular shifts and reversals of solutions. These symmetries are general and not limited to or tailored to TSP specifically.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们重新审视遗传算法（GA）在旅行商问题（TSP）中的应用，并介绍了一系列优于之前最先进技术的新型交叉算子。新颖的交叉算子旨在利用解空间中的对称性，这使我们能够更有效地保留表现良好的个体，即解的循环移位和反转的适应度不变性。这些对称性是一般性的，并不限于或专门针对 TSP 定制。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为CSRX的新交叉算子，用于改进遗传算法在旅行商问题（TSP）中的应用。
AI.motivation: 传统交叉算子未能有效利用解空间中的对称性（如循环移位和反转的适应度不变性），导致优良个体特征难以保留。
AI.method: 设计利用解空间对称性的新型交叉算子CSRX，通过保持适应度不变性来增强遗传算法的搜索效率。
AI.result: 实验表明，CSRX在TSP求解中优于现有最先进方法，且对称性特性具有通用性。
AI.conclusion: 通过引入对称性利用机制，CSRX显著提升了遗传算法在TSP中的性能，为其他优化问题提供新思路。

authors_text: Mohammad Azharuddin Sanpui
title: Externalities in Chore Division
title_zh: 家务部门的外部性
abstract: The chore division problem simulates the fair division of a heterogeneous, undesirable resource among several agents. In the fair division of chores, each agent only gets the disutility from its own piece. Agents may, however, also be concerned with the pieces given to other agents; these externalities naturally appear in fair division situations. We first demonstrate the generalization of the classical concepts of proportionality and envy-freeness while extending the classical model by taking externalities into account.
summary: The chore division problem simulates the fair division of a heterogeneous, undesirable resource among several agents. In the fair division of chores, each agent only gets the disutility from its own piece. Agents may, however, also be concerned with the pieces given to other agents; these externalities naturally appear in fair division situations. We first demonstrate the generalization of the classical concepts of proportionality and envy-freeness while extending the classical model by taking externalities into account.
abstract_zh: 杂务分配问题模拟了异构的、不需要的资源在多个代理之间的公平分配。在公平的家务分工中，每个智能体只能从自己的部分中获得负效用。然而，特工也可能关心给予其他特工的棋子；这些外部性自然会出现在公平分配的情况下。我们首先证明了比例性和无嫉妒性的经典概念的概括，同时通过考虑外部性来扩展经典模型。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了家务分配问题中外部性的影响，并扩展了传统公平分配的概念以考虑其他代理人的分配情况。
AI.motivation: 传统家务分配模型仅考虑代理人的自身不愉快程度，但现实中人们可能因他人分配到的不愉快任务而产生外部性，需更全面的公平性定义。
AI.method: 通过引入外部性因素，推广比例性与无嫉妒性的经典概念，构建考虑他人分配影响的公平分配模型。
AI.result: 提出了包含外部性的公平分配新标准，并分析了其在不同场景下的可行性与计算方法。
AI.conclusion: 考虑外部性的公平分配模型更符合现实情境，为家务分配问题提供了更精确的解决方案与研究方向。

ai_content_json.method: 通过引入外部性因素，推广比例性与无嫉妒性的经典概念，构建考虑他人分配影响的公平分配模型。
ai_content_json.result: 提出了包含外部性的公平分配新标准，并分析了其在不同场景下的可行性与计算方法。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 考虑外部性的公平分配模型更符合现实情境，为家务分配问题提供了更精确的解决方案与研究方向。

tldr: 本文研究了家务分配问题中外部性的影响，并扩展了传统公平分配的概念以考虑其他代理人的分配情况。

method: 通过引入外部性因素，推广比例性与无嫉妒性的经典概念，构建考虑他人分配影响的公平分配模型。
result: 提出了包含外部性的公平分配新标准，并分析了其在不同场景下的可行性与计算方法。

search_text: Externalities in Chore Division 家务部门的外部性 The chore division problem simulates the fair division of a heterogeneous, undesirable resource among several agents. In the fair division of chores, each agent only gets the disutility from its own piece. Agents may, however, also be concerned with the pieces given to other agents; these externalities naturally appear in fair division situations. We first demonstrate the generalization of the classical concepts of proportionality and envy-freeness while extending the classical model by taking externalities into account. 杂务分配问题模拟了异构的、不需要的资源在多个代理之间的公平分配。在公平的家务分工中，每个智能体只能从自己的部分中获得负效用。然而，特工也可能关心给予其他特工的棋子；这些外部性自然会出现在公平分配的情况下。我们首先证明了比例性和无嫉妒性的经典概念的概括，同时通过考虑外部性来扩展经典模型。 本文研究了家务分配问题中外部性的影响，并扩展了传统公平分配的概念以考虑其他代理人的分配情况。 Mohammad Azharuddin Sanpui cs.GT,cs.AI,cs.MA

authors_text: Leo Milecki,Vicky Kalogeiton,Sylvain Bodard,Dany Anglicheau,Jean-Michel Correas,Marc-Olivier Timsit,Maria Vakalopoulou
title: MEDIMP: 3D Medical Images with clinical Prompts from limited tabular data for renal transplantation
title_zh: MEDIMP：具有肾移植有限表格数据临床提示的 3D 医学图像
abstract: Renal transplantation emerges as the most effective solution for end-stage renal disease. Occurring from complex causes, a substantial risk of transplant chronic dysfunction persists and may lead to graft loss. Medical imaging plays a substantial role in renal transplant monitoring in clinical practice. However, graft supervision is multi-disciplinary, notably joining nephrology, urology, and radiology, while identifying robust biomarkers from such high-dimensional and complex data for prognosis is challenging. In this work, taking inspiration from the recent success of Large Language Models (LLMs), we propose MEDIMP -- Medical Images with clinical Prompts -- a model to learn meaningful multi-modal representations of renal transplant Dynamic Contrast-Enhanced Magnetic Resonance Imaging (DCE MRI) by incorporating structural clinicobiological data after translating them into text prompts. MEDIMP is based on contrastive learning from joint text-image paired embeddings to perform this challenging task. Moreover, we propose a framework that generates medical prompts using automatic textual data augmentations from LLMs. Our goal is to learn meaningful manifolds of renal transplant DCE MRI, interesting for the prognosis of the transplant or patient status (2, 3, and 4 years after the transplant), fully exploiting the limited available multi-modal data most efficiently. Extensive experiments and comparisons with other renal transplant representation learning methods with limited data prove the effectiveness of MEDIMP in a relevant clinical setting, giving new directions toward medical prompts. Our code is available at https://github.com/leomlck/MEDIMP.

Occurring from complex causes, a substantial risk of transplant chronic dysfunction persists and may lead to graft loss. Medical imaging plays a substantial role in renal transplant monitoring in clinical practice. However, graft supervision is multi-disciplinary, notably joining nephrology, urology, and radiology, while identifying robust biomarkers from such high-dimensional and complex data for prognosis is challenging. MEDIMP is based on contrastive learning from joint text-image paired embeddings to perform this challenging task. Moreover, we propose a framework that generates medical prompts using automatic textual data augmentations from LLMs. Our goal is to learn meaningful manifolds of renal transplant DCE MRI, interesting for the prognosis of the transplant or patient status (2, 3, and 4 years after the transplant), fully exploiting the limited available multi-modal data most efficiently. Extensive experiments and comparisons with other renal transplant representation learning methods with limited data prove the effectiveness of MEDIMP in a relevant clinical setting, giving new directions toward medical prompts. Our code is available at https://github.com/leomlck/MEDIMP.
abstract_zh: 肾移植成为治疗终末期肾病最有效的解决方案。由于复杂的原因，移植物慢性功能障碍的巨大风险持续存在，并可能导致移植物损失。医学成像在临床实践中的肾移植监测中发挥着重要作用。然而，移植物监督是多学科的，特别是肾病学、泌尿学和放射学的结合，而从如此高维和复杂的数据中识别可靠的生物标志物以进行预后是具有挑战性的。在这项工作中，从最近大语言模型（LLM）的成功中汲取灵感，我们提出了MEDIMP——带有临床提示的医学图像——一种模型，通过将结构性临床生物学数据翻译成文本提示后合并结构临床生物学数据来学习肾移植动态对比增强磁共振成像（DCE MRI）的有意义的多模态表示。 MEDIMP 基于联合文本图像配对嵌入的对比学习来执行这项具有挑战性的任务。此外，我们提出了一个框架，使用法学硕士的自动文本数据增强来生成医疗提示。我们的目标是学习有意义的肾移植 DCE MRI 流形，对移植预后或患者状态（移植后 2 年、3 年和 4 年）感兴趣，最有效地充分利用有限的可用多模态数据。大量实验以及与其他肾移植表征学习方法在有限数据下的比较证明了 MEDIMP 在相关临床环境中的有效性，为医疗提示提供了新的方向。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/leomlck/MEDIMP 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MEDIMP模型，通过结合临床文本提示与3D医学影像数据，解决肾移植后慢性功能障碍的预测问题。
AI.motivation: 肾移植后慢性功能障碍的预测面临多学科数据融合与高维影像分析的挑战，传统方法难以有效利用有限的多模态数据。
AI.method: 基于对比学习框架，将结构化临床数据转化为文本提示，与DCE-MRI影像进行联合嵌入学习，并通过LLM生成增强的医学提示以提升表征能力。
AI.result: 在有限多模态数据下，MEDIMP在移植后2/3/4年预后预测任务中优于现有方法，验证了临床提示与影像融合的有效性。
AI.conclusion: MEDIMP为医学影像与临床数据的联合分析提供了新范式，展示了基于提示的多模态学习在移植预后评估中的临床潜力。

authors_text: Stefan Scherzinger,Arne Roennau,Rüdiger Dillmann
title: Learning Human-Inspired Force Strategies for Robotic Assembly
title_zh: 学习机器人装配的受人类启发的力策略
comment: 8 pages, 8 figures. Submitted to the IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE) 2023
comments: 8 pages, 8 figures. Submitted to the IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE) 2023
abstract: The programming of robotic assembly tasks is a key component in manufacturing and automation. Force-sensitive assembly, however, often requires reactive strategies to handle slight changes in positioning and unforeseen part jamming. Learning such strategies from human performance is a promising approach, but faces two common challenges: the handling of low part clearances which is difficult to capture from demonstrations and learning intuitive strategies offline without access to the real hardware. We address these two challenges by learning probabilistic force strategies from data that are easily acquired offline in a robot-less simulation from human demonstrations with a joystick. We combine a Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) and a Mixture Density Network (MDN) to model human-inspired behavior in such a way that the learned strategies transfer easily onto real hardware. The experiments show a UR10e robot that completes a plastic assembly with clearances of less than 100 micrometers whose strategies were solely demonstrated in simulation.
summary: The programming of robotic assembly tasks is a key component in manufacturing and automation. Force-sensitive assembly, however, often requires reactive strategies to handle slight changes in positioning and unforeseen part jamming. Learning such strategies from human performance is a promising approach, but faces two common challenges: the handling of low part clearances which is difficult to capture from demonstrations and learning intuitive strategies offline without access to the real hardware. We address these two challenges by learning probabilistic force strategies from data that are easily acquired offline in a robot-less simulation from human demonstrations with a joystick. We combine a Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) and a Mixture Density Network (MDN) to model human-inspired behavior in such a way that the learned strategies transfer easily onto real hardware. The experiments show a UR10e robot that completes a plastic assembly with clearances of less than 100 micrometers whose strategies were solely demonstrated in simulation.
abstract_zh: 机器人装配任务的编程是制造和自动化的关键组成部分。然而，力敏感装配通常需要反应策略来处理定位的微小变化和不可预见的零件卡住。从人类表现中学习此类策略是一种很有前途的方法，但面临两个常见的挑战：处理难以从演示中捕获的低零件间隙，以及在无法访问真实硬件的情况下离线学习直观策略。我们通过从使用操纵杆进行人类演示的无机器人模拟中轻松离线获取的数据中学习概率力策略来解决这两个挑战。我们结合长短期记忆 (LSTM) 和混合密度网络 (MDN) 来模拟人类行为，以便将学习到的策略轻松转移到真实硬件上。实验表明，UR10e 机器人可以完成间隙小于 100 微米的塑料装配，其策略仅在模拟中得到验证。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种从人类演示中学习概率力策略的方法，解决机器人精密装配中微米级间隙处理和离线学习的难题。
AI.motivation: 传统机器人装配编程难以应对微米级零件间隙和突发卡顿问题，而直接从人类操作中学习又受限于硬件条件和数据获取难度。
AI.method: 结合LSTM与混合密度网络（MDN），通过离线模拟人类操纵杆演示数据，学习可迁移至真实硬件的概率力控制策略。
AI.result: UR10e机器人成功完成亚100微米间隙塑料件装配，策略完全源自仿真环境中的人类演示数据。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过仿真与深度学习结合，有效解决了精密装配中力控制策略的离线学习与硬件迁移难题。
